structure.name: Resource
structure.description: This is the base resource type for everything.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

structure.name: Account
structure.description: A financial tool for tracking value accrued for a particular purpose.  In the healthcare field, used to track charges for a patient, cost centers, etc.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: contained
items.description: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: modifierExtension
items.description:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: identifier
items.description: Unique identifier used to reference the account.  Might or might not be intended for human use (e.g. credit card number).

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates whether the account is presently used/usable or not.

items.name: type
items.description: Categorizes the account for reporting and searching purposes.

items.name: name
items.description: Name used for the account when displaying it to humans in reports, etc.

items.name: subject
items.description: Identifies the entity which incurs the expenses. While the immediate recipients of services or goods might be entities related to the subject, the expenses were ultimately incurred by the subject of the Account.

items.name: servicePeriod
items.description: The date range of services associated with this account.

items.name: coverage
items.description: The party(s) that are responsible for covering the payment of this account, and what order should they be applied to the account.

items.name: owner
items.description: Indicates the service area, hospital, department, etc. with responsibility for managing the Account.

items.name: description
items.description: Provides additional information about what the account tracks and how it is used.

items.name: guarantor
items.description: The parties responsible for balancing the account if other payment options fall short.

items.name: partOf
items.description: Reference to a parent Account.

structure.name: ActivityDefinition
structure.description: This resource allows for the definition of some activity to be performed, independent of a particular patient, practitioner, or other performance context.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules

Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this activity definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this activity definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the activity definition is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this activity definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the activity definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the activity definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. To provide a version consistent with the Decision Support Service specification, use the format Major.Minor.Revision (e.g. 1.0.0). For more information on versioning knowledge assets, refer to the Decision Support Service specification. Note that a version is required for non-experimental active assets.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the activity definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the activity definition.

items.name: subtitle
items.description: An explanatory or alternate title for the activity definition giving additional information about its content.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this activity definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this activity definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: subject[x]
items.description: A code or group definition that describes the intended subject of the activity being defined.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the activity definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the activity definition changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the activity definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the activity definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate activity definition instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the activity definition is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this activity definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: usage
items.description: A detailed description of how the activity definition is used from a clinical perspective.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the activity definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the activity definition.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the activity definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the activity. Topics provide a high-level categorization of the activity that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: library
items.description: A reference to a Library resource containing any formal logic used by the activity definition.

items.name: kind
items.description: A description of the kind of resource the activity definition is representing. For example, a MedicationRequest, a ServiceRequest, or a CommunicationRequest. Typically, but not always, this is a Request resource.

items.name: profile
items.description: A profile to which the target of the activity definition is expected to conform.

items.name: code
items.description: Detailed description of the type of activity; e.g. What lab test, what procedure, what kind of encounter.

items.name: intent
items.description: Indicates the level of authority/intentionality associated with the activity and where the request should fit into the workflow chain.

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the activity  should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: doNotPerform
items.description: Set this to true if the definition is to indicate that a particular activity should NOT be performed. If true, this element should be interpreted to reinforce a negative coding. For example NPO as a code with a doNotPerform of true would still indicate to NOT perform the action.

items.name: timing[x]
items.description: The period, timing or frequency upon which the described activity is to occur.

items.name: location
items.description: Identifies the facility where the activity will occur; e.g. home, hospital, specific clinic, etc.

items.name: participant
items.description: Indicates who should participate in performing the action described.

items.name: product[x]
items.description: Identifies the food, drug or other product being consumed or supplied in the activity.

items.name: quantity
items.description: Identifies the quantity expected to be consumed at once (per dose, per meal, etc.).

items.name: dosage
items.description: Provides detailed dosage instructions in the same way that they are described for MedicationRequest resources.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Indicates the sites on the subject's body where the procedure should be performed (I.e. the target sites).

items.name: specimenRequirement
items.description: Defines specimen requirements for the action to be performed, such as required specimens for a lab test.

items.name: observationRequirement
items.description: Defines observation requirements for the action to be performed, such as body weight or surface area.

items.name: observationResultRequirement
items.description: Defines the observations that are expected to be produced by the action.

items.name: transform
items.description: A reference to a StructureMap resource that defines a transform that can be executed to produce the intent resource using the ActivityDefinition instance as the input.

items.name: dynamicValue
items.description: Dynamic values that will be evaluated to produce values for elements of the resulting resource. For example, if the dosage of a medication must be computed based on the patient's weight, a dynamic value would be used to specify an expression that calculated the weight, and the path on the request resource that would contain the result.

structure.name: AdverseEvent
structure.description: Actual or  potential/avoided event causing unintended physical injury resulting from or contributed to by medical care, a research study or other healthcare setting factors that requires additional monitoring, treatment, or hospitalization, or that results in death.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this adverse event by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: actuality
items.description: Whether the event actually happened, or just had the potential to. Note that this is independent of whether anyone was affected or harmed or how severely.

items.name: category
items.description: The overall type of event, intended for search and filtering purposes.

items.name: event
items.description: This element defines the specific type of event that occurred or that was prevented from occurring.

items.name: subject
items.description: This subject or group impacted by the event.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which AdverseEvent was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: date
items.description: The date (and perhaps time) when the adverse event occurred.

items.name: detected
items.description: Estimated or actual date the AdverseEvent began, in the opinion of the reporter.

items.name: recordedDate
items.description: The date on which the existence of the AdverseEvent was first recorded.

items.name: resultingCondition
items.description: Includes information about the reaction that occurred as a result of exposure to a substance (for example, a drug or a chemical).

items.name: location
items.description: The information about where the adverse event occurred.

items.name: seriousness
items.description: Assessment whether this event was of real importance.

items.name: severity
items.description: Describes the severity of the adverse event, in relation to the subject. Contrast to AdverseEvent.seriousness - a severe rash might not be serious, but a mild heart problem is.

items.name: outcome
items.description: Describes the type of outcome from the adverse event.

items.name: recorder
items.description: Information on who recorded the adverse event.  May be the patient or a practitioner.

items.name: contributor
items.description: Parties that may or should contribute or have contributed information to the adverse event, which can consist of one or more activities.  Such information includes information leading to the decision to perform the activity and how to perform the activity (e.g. consultant), information that the activity itself seeks to reveal (e.g. informant of clinical history), or information about what activity was performed (e.g. informant witness).

items.name: suspectEntity
items.description: Describes the entity that is suspected to have caused the adverse event.

items.name: subjectMedicalHistory
items.description: AdverseEvent.subjectMedicalHistory.

items.name: referenceDocument
items.description: AdverseEvent.referenceDocument.

items.name: study
items.description: AdverseEvent.study.

structure.name: AllergyIntolerance
structure.description: Risk of harmful or undesirable, physiological response which is unique to an individual and associated with exposure to a substance.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules

Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this AllergyIntolerance by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: clinicalStatus
items.description: The clinical status of the allergy or intolerance.

items.name: verificationStatus
items.description: Assertion about certainty associated with the propensity, or potential risk, of a reaction to the identified substance (including pharmaceutical product).

items.name: type
items.description: Identification of the underlying physiological mechanism for the reaction risk.

items.name: category
items.description: Category of the identified substance.

items.name: criticality
items.description: Estimate of the potential clinical harm, or seriousness, of the reaction to the identified substance.

items.name: code
items.description: Code for an allergy or intolerance statement (either a positive or a negated/excluded statement).  This may be a code for a substance or pharmaceutical product that is considered to be responsible for the adverse reaction risk (e.g., "Latex"), an allergy or intolerance condition (e.g., "Latex allergy"), or a negated/excluded code for a specific substance or class (e.g., "No latex allergy") or a general or categorical negated statement (e.g.,  "No known allergy", "No known drug allergies").  Note: the substance for a specific reaction may be different from the substance identified as the cause of the risk, but it must be consistent with it. For instance, it may be a more specific substance (e.g. a brand medication) or a composite product that includes the identified substance. It must be clinically safe to only process the 'code' and ignore the 'reaction.substance'.  If a receiving system is unable to confirm that AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance falls within the semantic scope of AllergyIntolerance.code, then the receiving system should ignore AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance.

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient who has the allergy or intolerance.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The encounter when the allergy or intolerance was asserted.

items.name: onset[x]
items.description: Estimated or actual date,  date-time, or age when allergy or intolerance was identified.

items.name: recordedDate
items.description: The recordedDate represents when this particular AllergyIntolerance record was created in the system, which is often a system-generated date.

items.name: recorder
items.description: Individual who recorded the record and takes responsibility for its content.

items.name: asserter
items.description: The source of the information about the allergy that is recorded.

items.name: lastOccurrence
items.description: Represents the date and/or time of the last known occurrence of a reaction event.

items.name: note
items.description: Additional narrative about the propensity for the Adverse Reaction, not captured in other fields.

items.name: reaction
items.description: Details about each adverse reaction event linked to exposure to the identified substance.

structure.name: Appointment
structure.description: A booking of a healthcare event among patient(s), practitioner(s), related person(s) and/or device(s) for a specific date/time. This may result in one or more Encounter(s).

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: identifier
items.description: This records identifiers associated with this appointment concern that are defined by business processes and/or used to refer to it when a direct URL reference to the resource itself is not appropriate (e.g. in CDA documents, or in written / printed documentation).

items.name: status
items.description: The overall status of the Appointment. Each of the participants has their own participation status which indicates their involvement in the process, however this status indicates the shared status.

items.name: cancelationReason
items.description: The coded reason for the appointment being cancelled. This is often used in reporting/billing/futher processing to determine if further actions are required, or specific fees apply.

items.name: serviceCategory
items.description: A broad categorization of the service that is to be performed during this appointment.

items.name: serviceType
items.description: The specific service that is to be performed during this appointment.

items.name: specialty
items.description: The specialty of a practitioner that would be required to perform the service requested in this appointment.

items.name: appointmentType
items.description: The style of appointment or patient that has been booked in the slot (not service type).

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The coded reason that this appointment is being scheduled. This is more clinical than administrative.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Reason the appointment has been scheduled to take place, as specified using information from another resource. When the patient arrives and the encounter begins it may be used as the admission diagnosis. The indication will typically be a Condition (with other resources referenced in the evidence.detail), or a Procedure.

items.name: priority
items.description: The priority of the appointment. Can be used to make informed decisions if needing to re-prioritize appointments. (The iCal Standard specifies 0 as undefined, 1 as highest, 9 as lowest priority).

items.name: description
items.description: The brief description of the appointment as would be shown on a subject line in a meeting request, or appointment list. Detailed or expanded information should be put in the comment field.

items.name: supportingInformation
items.description: Additional information to support the appointment provided when making the appointment.

items.name: start
items.description: Date/Time that the appointment is to take place.

items.name: end
items.description: Date/Time that the appointment is to conclude.

items.name: minutesDuration
items.description: Number of minutes that the appointment is to take. This can be less than the duration between the start and end times.  For example, where the actual time of appointment is only an estimate or if a 30 minute appointment is being requested, but any time would work.  Also, if there is, for example, a planned 15 minute break in the middle of a long appointment, the duration may be 15 minutes less than the difference between the start and end.

items.name: slot
items.description: The slots from the participants' schedules that will be filled by the appointment.

items.name: created
items.description: The date that this appointment was initially created. This could be different to the meta.lastModified value on the initial entry, as this could have been before the resource was created on the FHIR server, and should remain unchanged over the lifespan of the appointment.

items.name: comment
items.description: Additional comments about the appointment.

items.name: patientInstruction
items.description: While Appointment.comment contains information for internal use, Appointment.patientInstructions is used to capture patient facing information about the Appointment (e.g. please bring your referral or fast from 8pm night before).

items.name: basedOn
items.description: The service request this appointment is allocated to assess (e.g. incoming referral or procedure request).

items.name: participant
items.description: List of participants involved in the appointment.

items.name: requestedPeriod
items.description:
A set of date ranges (potentially including times) that the appointment is preferred to be scheduled within.

The duration (usually in minutes) could also be provided to indicate the length of the appointment to fill and populate the start/end times for the actual allocated time. However, in other situations the duration may be calculated by the scheduling system.

structure.name: AppointmentResponse
structure.description: A reply to an appointment request for a patient and/or practitioner(s), such as a confirmation or rejection.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta

items.description: Date/Time that the appointment is to take place, or requested new start time.

items.name: end
items.description: This may be either the same as the appointment request to confirm the details of the appointment, or alternately a new time to request a re-negotiation of the end time.

items.name: participantType
items.description: Role of participant in the appointment.

items.name: actor
items.description: A Person, Location, HealthcareService, or Device that is participating in the appointment.

items.name: participantStatus
items.description: Participation status of the participant. When the status is declined or tentative if the start/end times are different to the appointment, then these times should be interpreted as a requested time change. When the status is accepted, the times can either be the time of the appointment (as a confirmation of the time) or can be empty.

items.name: comment

items.description: This element identifies another resource that can be used as a proxy of the security sensitivity to use when deciding and enforcing access control rules for the Binary resource. Given that the Binary resource contains very few elements that can be used to determine the sensitivity of the data and relationships to individuals, the referenced resource stands in as a proxy equivalent for this purpose. This referenced resource may be related to the Binary (e.g. Media, DocumentReference), or may be some non-related Resource purely as a security proxy. E.g. to identify that the binary resource relates to a patient, and access should only be granted to applications that have access to the patient.

items.name: productCategory
items.description: Broad category of this product.

items.description: A code that identifies the kind of this biologically derived product (SNOMED Ctcode).

items.name: status
items.description: Whether the product is currently available.

items.name: request
items.description: Procedure request to obtain this biologically derived product.

items.name: quantity
items.description: Number of discrete units within this product.

items.name: parent
items.description: Parent product (if any).

items.name: collection
items.description: How this product was collected.

items.name: processing
items.description: Any processing of the product during collection that does not change the fundamental nature of the product. For example adding anti-coagulants during the collection of Peripheral Blood Stem Cells.

items.name: manipulation
items.description: Any manipulation of product post-collection that is intended to alter the product.  For example a buffy-coat enrichment or CD8 reduction of Peripheral Blood Stem Cells to make it more suitable for infusion.

items.name: storage
items.description: Product storage.

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this capability statement when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this capability statement is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the capability statement is stored on different servers.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the capability statement when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the capability statement author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the capability statement. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the capability statement.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this capability statement. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this capability statement is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the capability statement was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the capability statement changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the capability statement.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the capability statement from a consumer's perspective. Typically, this is used when the capability statement describes a desired rather than an actual solution, for example as a formal expression of requirements as part of an RFP.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate capability statement instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the capability statement is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this capability statement is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the capability statement and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the capability statement.

items.name: kind
items.description: The way that this statement is intended to be used, to describe an actual running instance of software, a particular product (kind, not instance of software) or a class of implementation (e.g. a desired purchase).

items.name: instantiates
items.description: Reference to a canonical URL of another CapabilityStatement that this software implements. This capability statement is a published API description that corresponds to a business service. The server may actually implement a subset of the capability statement it claims to implement, so the capability statement must specify the full capability details.

items.name: imports
items.description: Reference to a canonical URL of another CapabilityStatement that this software adds to. The capability statement automatically includes everything in the other statement, and it is not duplicated, though the server may repeat the same resources, interactions and operations to add additional details to them.

items.name: software
items.description: Software that is covered by this capability statement.  It is used when the capability statement describes the capabilities of a particular software version, independent of an installation.

items.name: implementation
items.description: Identifies a specific implementation instance that is described by the capability statement - i.e. a particular installation, rather than the capabilities of a software program.

items.name: fhirVersion
items.description: The version of the FHIR specification that this CapabilityStatement describes (which SHALL be the same as the FHIR version of the CapabilityStatement itself). There is no default value.

items.name: format
items.description: A list of the formats supported by this implementation using their content types.

items.name: patchFormat
items.description: A list of the patch formats supported by this implementation using their content types.

items.name: implementationGuide
items.description: A list of implementation guides that the server does (or should) support in their entirety.

items.name: rest
items.description: A definition of the restful capabilities of the solution, if any.

items.name: messaging
items.description: A description of the messaging capabilities of the solution.

items.name: document
items.description: A document definition.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this care plan by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, questionnaire or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this CarePlan.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, questionnaire or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this CarePlan.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A care plan that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this care plan.

items.name: replaces
items.description: Completed or terminated care plan whose function is taken by this new care plan.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger care plan of which this particular care plan is a component or step.

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates whether the plan is currently being acted upon, represents future intentions or is now a historical record.

items.name: intent
items.description: Indicates the level of authority/intentionality associated with the care plan and where the care plan fits into the workflow chain.

items.name: category
items.description: Identifies what "kind" of plan this is to support differentiation between multiple co-existing plans; e.g. "Home health", "psychiatric", "asthma", "disease management", "wellness plan", etc.

items.name: title
items.description: Human-friendly name for the care plan.

items.name: description
items.description: A description of the scope and nature of the plan.

items.name: subject
items.description: Identifies the patient or group whose intended care is described by the plan.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this CarePlan was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: period
items.description: Indicates when the plan did (or is intended to) come into effect and end.

items.name: created
items.description: Represents when this particular CarePlan record was created in the system, which is often a system-generated date.

items.name: author
items.description: When populated, the author is responsible for the care plan.  The care plan is attributed to the author.

items.name: contributor
items.description: Identifies the individual(s) or organization who provided the contents of the care plan.

items.name: careTeam
items.description: Identifies all people and organizations who are expected to be involved in the care envisioned by this plan.

items.name: addresses
items.description: Identifies the conditions/problems/concerns/diagnoses/etc. whose management and/or mitigation are handled by this plan.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Identifies portions of the patient's record that specifically influenced the formation of the plan.  These might include comorbidities, recent procedures, limitations, recent assessments, etc.

items.name: goal
items.description: Describes the intended objective(s) of carrying out the care plan.

items.name: activity
items.description: Identifies a planned action to occur as part of the plan.  For example, a medication to be used, lab tests to perform, self-monitoring, education, etc.

items.name: note
items.description: General notes about the care plan not covered elsewhere.

items.description: A label for human use intended to distinguish like teams.  E.g. the "red" vs. "green" trauma teams.

items.name: subject
items.description: Identifies the patient or group whose intended care is handled by the team.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this CareTeam was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: period
items.description: Indicates when the team did (or is intended to) come into effect and end.

items.name: participant
items.description: Identifies all people and organizations who are expected to be involved in the care team.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes why the care team exists.

items.name: reasonReference

items.name: managingOrganization
items.description: The organization responsible for the care team.

items.name: telecom
items.description: A central contact detail for the care team (that applies to all members).

items.name: note

items.name: type
items.description: The type of item - medication, device, service, protocol or other.

items.name: orderable
items.description: Whether the entry represents an orderable item.

items.name: referencedItem
items.description: The item in a catalog or definition.

items.name: additionalIdentifier
items.description: Used in supporting related concepts, e.g. NDC to RxNorm.

items.name: classification
items.description: Classes of devices, or ATC for medication.

items.name: status
items.description: Used to support catalog exchange even for unsupported products, e.g. getting list of medications even if not prescribable.

items.name: validityPeriod
items.description: The time period in which this catalog entry is expected to be active.

items.name: validTo
items.description: The date until which this catalog entry is expected to be active.

items.name: lastUpdated
items.description: Typically date of issue is different from the beginning of the validity. This can be used to see when an item was last updated.

items.name: additionalCharacteristic
items.description: Used for examplefor Out of Formulary, or any specifics.

items.name: additionalClassification
items.description: User for example for ATC classification, or.

items.name: relatedEntry
items.description: Used for example, to point to a substance, or to a device used to administer a medication.

Main Usage of the ChargeItem is to enable the billing process and internal cost allocation.

items.name: definitionUri
items.description: References the (external) source of pricing information, rules of application for the code this ChargeItem uses.

items.name: definitionCanonical
items.description: References the source of pricing information, rules of application for the code this ChargeItem uses.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the ChargeItem.

items.name: partOf
items.description: ChargeItems can be grouped to larger ChargeItems covering the whole set.

items.name: code
items.description: A code that identifies the charge, like a billing code.

items.name: subject
items.description: The individual or set of individuals the action is being or was performed on.

items.name: context
items.description: The encounter or episode of care that establishes the context for this event.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: Date/time(s) or duration when the charged service was applied.

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates who or what performed or participated in the charged service.

items.name: performingOrganization
items.description: The organization requesting the service.

items.name: requestingOrganization
items.description: The organization performing the service.

items.name: costCenter
items.description: The financial cost center permits the tracking of charge attribution.

items.name: quantity
items.description: Quantity of which the charge item has been serviced.

items.name: bodysite
items.description: The anatomical location where the related service has been applied.

items.name: factorOverride
items.description: Factor overriding the factor determined by the rules associated with the code.

items.name: priceOverride
items.description: Total price of the charge overriding the list price associated with the code.

items.name: overrideReason
items.description: If the list price or the rule-based factor associated with the code is overridden, this attribute can capture a text to indicate the  reason for this action.

items.name: enterer
items.description: The device, practitioner, etc. who entered the charge item.

items.name: enteredDate
items.description: Date the charge item was entered.

items.name: reason
items.description: Describes why the event occurred in coded or textual form.

items.name: service
items.description: Indicated the rendered service that caused this charge.

items.name: product[x]
items.description: Identifies the device, food, drug or other product being charged either by type code or reference to an instance.

items.name: account
items.description: Account into which this ChargeItems belongs.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the event by the performer, subject or other participants.

items.name: supportingInformation
items.description: Further information supporting this charge.

items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this charge item definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this charge item definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the charge item definition is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this charge item definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the charge item definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the charge item definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. items.name: title

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger definition of which this particular definition is a component or step.

items.name: replaces
items.description: As new versions of a protocol or guideline are defined, allows identification of what versions are replaced by a new instance.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the ChargeItemDefinition.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this charge item definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the charge item definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the charge item definition changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the charge item definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the charge item definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate charge item definition instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the charge item definition is intended to be used.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the charge item definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the charge item definition.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the charge item definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: code
items.description: The defined billing details in this resource pertain to the given billing code.

items.name: instance
items.description: The defined billing details in this resource pertain to the given product instance(s).

items.name: applicability
items.description: Expressions that describe applicability criteria for the billing code.

items.name: propertyGroup
items.description: Group of properties which are applicable under the same conditions. If no applicability rules are established for the group, then all properties always apply.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this claim.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: The category of claim, e.g. oral, pharmacy, vision, institutional, professional.

items.name: subType
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: use
items.description: A code to indicate whether the nature of the request is: to request adjudication of products and services previously rendered; or requesting authorization and adjudication for provision in the future; or requesting the non-binding adjudication of the listed products and services which could be provided in the future.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party to whom the professional services and/or products have been supplied or are being considered and for whom actual or forecast reimbursement is sought.

items.name: billablePeriod
items.description: The period for which charges are being submitted.

items.name: created
items.description: The date this resource was created.

items.name: enterer
items.description: Individual who created the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The Insurer who is target of the request.

items.name: provider
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: priority
items.description: The provider-required urgency of processing the request. Typical values include: stat, routine deferred.

items.name: fundsReserve
items.description: A code to indicate whether and for whom funds are to be reserved for future claims.

items.name: related
items.description: Other claims which are related to this claim such as prior submissions or claims for related services or for the same event.

items.name: prescription
items.description: Prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy, device or vision products.

items.name: originalPrescription
items.description: Original prescription which has been superseded by this prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy services, medications or products.

items.name: payee
items.description: The party to be reimbursed for cost of the products and services according to the terms of the policy.

items.name: referral
items.description: A reference to a referral resource.

items.name: facility
items.description: Facility where the services were provided.

items.name: careTeam
items.description: The members of the team who provided the products and services.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional information codes regarding exceptions, special considerations, the condition, situation, prior or concurrent issues.

items.name: diagnosis
items.description: Information about diagnoses relevant to the claim items.

items.name: procedure
items.description: Procedures performed on the patient relevant to the billing items with the claim.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: accident
items.description: Details of an accident which resulted in injuries which required the products and services listed in the claim.

items.name: item
items.description: A claim line. Either a simple  product or service or a 'group' of details which can each be a simple items or groups of sub-details.

items.name: total
items.description: The total value of the all the items in the claim.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: subType
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: use
items.description: A code to indicate whether the nature of the request is: to request adjudication of products and services previously rendered; or requesting authorization and adjudication for provision in the future; or requesting the non-binding adjudication of the listed products and services which could be provided in the future.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party to whom the professional services and/or products have been supplied or are being considered and for whom actual for facast reimbursement is sought.

items.name: requestor
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: request
items.description: Original request resource reference.

items.name: outcome
items.description: The outcome of the claim, predetermination, or preauthorization processing.

items.name: disposition
items.description: A human readable description of the status of the adjudication.

items.name: preAuthRef
items.description: Reference from the Insurer which is used in later communications which refers to this adjudication.

items.name: preAuthPeriod
items.description: The time frame during which this authorization is effective.

items.name: payeeType
items.description: Type of Party to be reimbursed: subscriber, provider, other.

items.name: item
items.description: A claim line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of details which can also be a simple items or groups of sub-details.

items.name: total

items.name: formCode
items.description: A code for the form to be used for printing the content.

items.name: form
items.description: The actual form, by reference or inclusion, for printing the content or an EOB.

items.name: processNote
items.description: A note that describes or explains adjudication results in a human readable form.

items.name: communicationRequest
items.description: Request for additional supporting or authorizing information.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: error

structure.name: ClinicalImpression
structure.description: A record of a clinical assessment performed to determine what problem(s) may affect the patient and before planning the treatments or management strategies that are best to manage a patient's condition. Assessments are often 1:1 with a clinical consultation / encounter,  but this varies greatly depending on the clinical workflow. This resource is called "ClinicalImpression" rather than "ClinicalAssessment" to avoid confusion with the recording of assessment tools such as Apgar score.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.description: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this clinical impression by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: status
items.description: Identifies the workflow status of the assessment.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current state of the ClinicalImpression.

items.name: code
items.description: Categorizes the type of clinical assessment performed.

items.name: description
items.description: A summary of the context and/or cause of the assessment - why / where it was performed, and what patient events/status prompted it.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient or group of individuals assessed as part of this record.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this ClinicalImpression was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: effective[x]
items.description: The point in time or period over which the subject was assessed.

items.name: date
items.description: Indicates when the documentation of the assessment was complete.

items.name: assessor
items.description: The clinician performing the assessment.

items.name: previous
items.description: A reference to the last assessment that was conducted on this patient. Assessments are often/usually ongoing in nature; a care provider (practitioner or team) will make new assessments on an ongoing basis as new data arises or the patient's conditions changes.

items.name: problem
items.description: A list of the relevant problems/conditions for a patient.

items.name: investigation
items.description: One or more sets of investigations (signs, symptoms, etc.). The actual grouping of investigations varies greatly depending on the type and context of the assessment. These investigations may include data generated during the assessment process, or data previously generated and recorded that is pertinent to the outcomes.

items.name: protocol
items.description: Reference to a specific published clinical protocol that was followed during this assessment, and/or that provides evidence in support of the diagnosis.

items.name: summary
items.description: A text summary of the investigations and the diagnosis.

items.name: finding
items.description: Specific findings or diagnoses that were considered likely or relevant to ongoing treatment.

items.name: prognosisCodeableConcept
items.description: Estimate of likely outcome.

items.name: prognosisReference
items.description: RiskAssessment expressing likely outcome.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Information supporting the clinical impression.

items.name: note
items.description: Commentary about the impression, typically recorded after the impression itself was made, though supplemental notes by the original author could also appear.

structure.name: CodeSystem

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this code system when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this code system is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the code system is stored on different servers. This is used in [Coding](datatypes.html#Coding).system.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this code system when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the code system when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the code system author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. This is used in [Coding](datatypes.html#Coding).version.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the code system. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the code system.

items.name: status
items.description: The date (and optionally time) when the code system resource was created or revised.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this code system is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the code system was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the code system changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the code system.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the code system from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate code system instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the code system is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this code system is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the code system and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the code system.

items.name: caseSensitive
items.description: If code comparison is case sensitive when codes within this system are compared to each other.

items.name: valueSet
items.description: Canonical reference to the value set that contains the entire code system.

items.name: hierarchyMeaning
items.description: The meaning of the hierarchy of concepts as represented in this resource.

items.name: compositional
items.description: The code system defines a compositional (post-coordination) grammar.

items.name: versionNeeded
items.description: This flag is used to signify that the code system does not commit to concept permanence across versions. If true, a version must be specified when referencing this code system.

items.name: content
items.description: The extent of the content of the code system (the concepts and codes it defines) are represented in this resource instance.

items.name: supplements
items.description: The canonical URL of the code system that this code system supplement is adding designations and properties to.

items.name: count
items.description: The total number of concepts defined by the code system. Where the code system has a compositional grammar, the basis of this count is defined by the system steward.

items.name: filter
items.description: A filter that can be used in a value set compose statement when selecting concepts using a filter.

items.name: property
items.description: A property defines an additional slot through which additional information can be provided about a concept.

items.name: concept
items.description: Concepts that are in the code system. The concept definitions are inherently hierarchical, but the definitions must be consulted to determine what the meanings of the hierarchical relationships are.

structure.name: Communication
structure.description: An occurrence of information being transmitted; e.g. an alert that was sent to a responsible provider, a public health agency that was notified about a reportable condition.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this communication by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Communication.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Communication.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: An order, proposal or plan fulfilled in whole or in part by this Communication.

items.name: partOf
items.description: Part of this action.

items.name: inResponseTo
items.description: Prior communication that this communication is in response to.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the transmission.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current state of the Communication.

items.name: category
items.description: The type of message conveyed such as alert, notification, reminder, instruction, etc.

items.name: priority
items.description: Characterizes how quickly the planned or in progress communication must be addressed. Includes concepts such as stat, urgent, routine.

items.name: medium
items.description: A channel that was used for this communication (e.g. email, fax).

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient or group that was the focus of this communication.

items.name: topic
items.description: Description of the purpose/content, similar to a subject line in an email.

items.name: about
items.description: Other resources that pertain to this communication and to which this communication should be associated.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this Communication was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: sent
items.description: The time when this communication was sent.

items.name: received
items.description: The time when this communication arrived at the destination.

items.name: recipient
items.description: The entity (e.g. person, organization, clinical information system, care team or device) which was the target of the communication. If receipts need to be tracked by an individual, a separate resource instance will need to be created for each recipient.  Multiple recipient communications are intended where either receipts are not tracked (e.g. a mass mail-out) or a receipt is captured in aggregate (all emails confirmed received by a particular time).

items.name: sender
items.description: The entity (e.g. person, organization, clinical information system, or device) which was the source of the communication.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The reason or justification for the communication.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource whose existence justifies this communication.

items.name: payload
items.description: Text, attachment(s), or resource(s) that was communicated to the recipient.

items.name: note
items.description: Additional notes or commentary about the communication by the sender, receiver or other interested parties.

structure.name: CommunicationRequest
structure.description: A request to convey information; e.g. the CDS system proposes that an alert be sent to a responsible provider, the CDS system proposes that the public health agency be notified about a reportable condition.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this communication request by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A plan or proposal that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this request.

items.name: replaces
items.description: Completed or terminated request(s) whose function is taken by this new request.

items.name: groupIdentifier
items.description: A shared identifier common to all requests that were authorized more or less simultaneously by a single author, representing the identifier of the requisition, prescription or similar form.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the proposal or order.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current state of the CommunicationRequest.

items.name: category
items.description: The type of message to be sent such as alert, notification, reminder, instruction, etc.

items.name: priority
items.description: Characterizes how quickly the proposed act must be initiated. Includes concepts such as stat, urgent, routine.

items.name: doNotPerform
items.description: If true indicates that the CommunicationRequest is asking for the specified action to *not* occur.

items.name: medium
items.description: A channel that was used for this communication (e.g. email, fax).

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient or group that is the focus of this communication request.

items.name: about
items.description: Other resources that pertain to this communication request and to which this communication request should be associated.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this CommunicationRequest was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: payload
items.description: Text, attachment(s), or resource(s) to be communicated to the recipient.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: The time when this communication is to occur.

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: For draft requests, indicates the date of initial creation.  For requests with other statuses, indicates the date of activation.

items.name: requester
items.description: The device, individual, or organization who initiated the request and has responsibility for its activation.

items.name: recipient
items.description: The entity (e.g. person, organization, clinical information system, device, group, or care team) which is the intended target of the communication.

items.name: sender
items.description: The entity (e.g. person, organization, clinical information system, or device) which is to be the source of the communication.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes why the request is being made in coded or textual form.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource whose existence justifies this request.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the request by the requester, sender, recipient, subject or other participants.

items.name: code

items.name: search

items.name: resource
items.description: Information about how a resource is related to the compartment.

structure.name: Composition
structure.description: A set of healthcare-related information that is assembled together into a single logical package that provides a single coherent statement of meaning, establishes its own context and that has clinical attestation with regard to who is making the statement. A Composition defines the structure and narrative content necessary for a document. However, a Composition alone does not constitute a document. Rather, the Composition must be the first entry in a Bundle where Bundle.type=document, and any other resources referenced from Composition must be included as subsequent entries in the Bundle (for example Patient, Practitioner, Encounter, etc.).

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta

items.name: identifier
items.description: A version-independent identifier for the Composition. This identifier stays constant as the composition is changed over time.

items.name: status
items.description: The workflow/clinical status of this composition. The status is a marker for the clinical standing of the document.

items.name: type
items.description: Specifies the particular kind of composition (e.g. History and Physical, Discharge Summary, Progress Note). This usually equates to the purpose of making the composition.

items.name: category
items.description: A categorization for the type of the composition - helps for indexing and searching. This may be implied by or derived from the code specified in the Composition Type.

items.name: subject
items.description: Who or what the composition is about. The composition can be about a person, (patient or healthcare practitioner), a device (e.g. a machine) or even a group of subjects (such as a document about a herd of livestock, or a set of patients that share a common exposure).

items.name: encounter
items.description: Describes the clinical encounter or type of care this documentation is associated with.

items.name: date
items.description: The composition editing time, when the composition was last logically changed by the author.

items.name: author
items.description: Identifies who is responsible for the information in the composition, not necessarily who typed it in.

items.name: title
items.description: Official human-readable label for the composition.

items.name: confidentiality
items.description: The code specifying the level of confidentiality of the Composition.

items.name: attester
items.description: A participant who has attested to the accuracy of the composition/document.

items.name: custodian
items.description: Identifies the organization or group who is responsible for ongoing maintenance of and access to the composition/document information.

items.name: relatesTo
items.description: Relationships that this composition has with other compositions or documents that already exist.

items.name: event
items.description: The clinical service, such as a colonoscopy or an appendectomy, being documented.

items.name: section
items.description: The root of the sections that make up the composition.

structure.name: ConceptMap
structure.description: A statement of relationships from one set of concepts to one or more other concepts - either concepts in code systems, or data element/data element concepts, or classes in class models.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.
