items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this concept map is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the concept map is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this concept map when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the concept map author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the concept map. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the concept map.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this concept map. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this concept map is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the concept map was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the concept map changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the concept map.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the concept map from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate concept map instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the concept map is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this concept map is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the concept map and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the concept map.

items.name: source[x]
items.description: Identifier for the source value set that contains the concepts that are being mapped and provides context for the mappings.

items.name: target[x]
items.description: The target value set provides context for the mappings. Note that the mapping is made between concepts, not between value sets, but the value set provides important context about how the concept mapping choices are made.

items.name: group
items.description: A group of mappings that all have the same source and target system.

structure.name: Condition
structure.description: A clinical condition, problem, diagnosis, or other event, situation, issue, or clinical concept that has risen to a level of concern.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this condition by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: clinicalStatus
items.description: The clinical status of the condition.

items.name: verificationStatus
items.description: The verification status to support the clinical status of the condition.

items.name: category
items.description: A category assigned to the condition.

items.name: severity
items.description: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.

items.name: code
items.description: Identification of the condition, problem or diagnosis.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: The anatomical location where this condition manifests itself.

items.name: subject
items.description: Indicates the patient or group who the condition record is associated with.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this Condition was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: onset[x]
items.description: Estimated or actual date or date-time  the condition began, in the opinion of the clinician.

items.name: abatement[x]
items.description: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission. This is called "abatement" because of the many overloaded connotations associated with "remission" or "resolution" - Conditions are never really resolved, but they can abate.

items.name: recordedDate
items.description: The recordedDate represents when this particular Condition record was created in the system, which is often a system-generated date.

items.name: recorder
items.description: Individual who recorded the record and takes responsibility for its content.

items.name: asserter
items.description: Individual who is making the condition statement.

items.name: stage
items.description: Clinical stage or grade of a condition. May include formal severity assessments.

items.name: evidence
items.description: Supporting evidence / manifestations that are the basis of the Condition's verification status, such as evidence that confirmed or refuted the condition.

items.name: note
items.description: Additional information about the Condition. This is a general notes/comments entry  for description of the Condition, its diagnosis and prognosis.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Unique identifier for this copy of the Consent Statement.

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates the current state of this consent.

items.name: scope
items.description: A selector of the type of consent being presented: ADR, Privacy, Treatment, Research.  This list is now extensible.

items.name: category
items.description: A classification of the type of consents found in the statement. This element supports indexing and retrieval of consent statements.

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient/healthcare consumer to whom this consent applies.

items.name: dateTime
items.description: When this  Consent was issued / created / indexed.

items.name: performer
items.description: Either the Grantor, which is the entity responsible for granting the rights listed in a Consent Directive or the Grantee, which is the entity responsible for complying with the Consent Directive, including any obligations or limitations on authorizations and enforcement of prohibitions.

items.name: organization
items.description: The organization that manages the consent, and the framework within which it is executed.

items.name: source[x]
items.description: The source on which this consent statement is based. The source might be a scanned original paper form, or a reference to a consent that links back to such a source, a reference to a document repository (e.g. XDS) that stores the original consent document.

items.name: policy
items.description: The references to the policies that are included in this consent scope. Policies may be organizational, but are often defined jurisdictionally, or in law.

items.name: policyRule
items.description: A reference to the specific base computable regulation or policy.

items.name: verification
items.description: Whether a treatment instruction (e.g. artificial respiration yes or no) was verified with the patient, his/her family or another authorized person.

items.name: provision
items.description: An exception to the base policy of this consent. An exception can be an addition or removal of access permissions.

items.name: type

Provides additional information about its content within the context of the Contract's scope to distinguish the kinds of systems that would be interested in the contract.

items.name: subType

items.name: contentDefinition

items.name: supportingInfo

items.name: relevantHistory

The Provence.entity indicates the target that was changed in the update. http://build.fhir.org/provenance-definitions.html#Provenance.entity.

items.name: friendly
items.description: The "patient friendly language" versionof the Contract in whole or in parts. "Patient friendly language" means the representation of the Contract and Contract Provisions in a manner that is readily accessible and understandable by a layperson in accordance with best practices for communication styles that ensure that those agreeing to or signing the Contract understand the roles, actions, obligations, responsibilities, and implication of the agreement.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this coverage.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: The type of coverage: social program, medical plan, accident coverage (workers compensation, auto), group health or payment by an individual or organization.

items.name: policyHolder
items.description: The party who 'owns' the insurance policy.

items.name: subscriber
items.description: The party who has signed-up for or 'owns' the contractual relationship to the policy or to whom the benefit of the policy for services rendered to them or their family is due.

items.name: subscriberId
items.description: The insurer assigned ID for the Subscriber.

items.name: beneficiary
items.description: The party who benefits from the insurance coverage; the patient when products and/or services are provided.

items.name: dependent
items.description: A unique identifier for a dependent under the coverage.

items.name: relationship
items.description: The relationship of beneficiary (patient) to the subscriber.

items.name: period
items.description: Time period during which the coverage is in force. A missing start date indicates the start date isn't known, a missing end date means the coverage is continuing to be in force.

items.name: payor
items.description: The program or plan underwriter or payor including both insurance and non-insurance agreements, such as patient-pay agreements.

items.name: class
items.description: A suite of underwriter specific classifiers.

items.name: order
items.description: The order of applicability of this coverage relative to other coverages which are currently in force. Note, there may be gaps in the numbering and this does not imply primary, secondary etc. as the specific positioning of coverages depends upon the episode of care.

items.name: network
items.description: The insurer-specific identifier for the insurer-defined network of providers to which the beneficiary may seek treatment which will be covered at the 'in-network' rate, otherwise 'out of network' terms and conditions apply.

items.name: costToBeneficiary
items.description: A suite of codes indicating the cost category and associated amount which have been detailed in the policy and may have been  included on the health card.

items.name: subrogation
items.description: When 'subrogation=true' this insurance instance has been included not for adjudication but to provide insurers with the details to recover costs.

items.name: contract
items.description: The policy(s) which constitute this insurance coverage.

structure.name: CoverageEligibilityRequest
structure.description: The CoverageEligibilityRequest provides patient and insurance coverage information to an insurer for them to respond, in the form of an CoverageEligibilityResponse, with information regarding whether the stated coverage is valid and in-force and optionally to provide the insurance details of the policy.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this coverage eligiblity request.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: priority
items.description: When the requestor expects the processor to complete processing.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Code to specify whether requesting: prior authorization requirements for some service categories or billing codes; benefits for coverages specified or discovered; discovery and return of coverages for the patient; and/or validation that the specified coverage is in-force at the date/period specified or 'now' if not specified.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party who is the beneficiary of the supplied coverage and for whom eligibility is sought.

items.name: serviced[x]
items.description: The date or dates when the enclosed suite of services were performed or completed.

items.name: created
items.description: The date when this resource was created.

items.name: enterer
items.description: Person who created the request.

items.name: provider
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the request.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The Insurer who issued the coverage in question and is the recipient of the request.

items.name: facility
items.description: Facility where the services are intended to be provided.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional information codes regarding exceptions, special considerations, the condition, situation, prior or concurrent issues.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services.

items.name: item
items.description: Service categories or billable services for which benefit details and/or an authorization prior to service delivery may be required by the payor.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this coverage eligiblity request.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Code to specify whether requesting: prior authorization requirements for some service categories or billing codes; benefits for coverages specified or discovered; discovery and return of coverages for the patient; and/or validation that the specified coverage is in-force at the date/period specified or 'now' if not specified.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party who is the beneficiary of the supplied coverage and for whom eligibility is sought.

items.name: serviced[x]
items.description: The date or dates when the enclosed suite of services were performed or completed.

items.name: created
items.description: The date this resource was created.

items.name: requestor
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the request.

items.name: request
items.description: Reference to the original request resource.

items.name: outcome
items.description: The outcome of the request processing.

items.name: disposition
items.description: A human readable description of the status of the adjudication.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The Insurer who issued the coverage in question and is the author of the response.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services.

items.name: preAuthRef
items.description: A reference from the Insurer to which these services pertain to be used on further communication and as proof that the request occurred.

items.name: form
items.description: A code for the form to be used for printing the content.

items.name: error
items.description: Errors encountered during the processing of the request.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifier associated with the detected issue record.

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates the status of the detected issue.

items.name: code
items.description: Identifies the general type of issue identified.

items.name: severity
items.description: Indicates the degree of importance associated with the identified issue based on the potential impact on the patient.

items.name: patient
items.description: Indicates the patient whose record the detected issue is associated with.

items.name: identified[x]
items.description: The date or period when the detected issue was initially identified.

items.name: author
items.description: Individual or device responsible for the issue being raised.  For example, a decision support application or a pharmacist conducting a medication review.

items.name: implicated
items.description: Indicates the resource representing the current activity or proposed activity that is potentially problematic.

items.name: evidence
items.description: Supporting evidence or manifestations that provide the basis for identifying the detected issue such as a GuidanceResponse or MeasureReport.

items.name: detail
items.description: A textual explanation of the detected issue.

items.name: reference
items.description: The literature, knowledge-base or similar reference that describes the propensity for the detected issue identified.

items.name: mitigation
items.description: Indicates an action that has been taken or is committed to reduce or eliminate the likelihood of the risk identified by the detected issue from manifesting.  Can also reflect an observation of known mitigating factors that may reduce/eliminate the need for any action.

structure.name: Device
structure.description: A type of a manufactured item that is used in the provision of healthcare without being substantially changed through that activity. The device may be a medical or non-medical device.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Unique instance identifiers assigned to a device by manufacturers other organizations or owners.

items.name: definition
items.description: The reference to the definition for the device.

items.name: udiCarrier
items.description: Unique device identifier (UDI) assigned to device label or package.  Note that the Device may include multiple udiCarriers as it either may include just the udiCarrier for the jurisdiction it is sold, or for multiple jurisdictions it could have been sold.

items.name: status
items.description: Status of the Device availability.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Reason for the dtatus of the Device availability.

items.name: distinctIdentifier
items.description: The distinct identification string as required by regulation for a human cell, tissue, or cellular and tissue-based product.

items.name: manufacturer
items.description: A name of the manufacturer.

items.name: manufactureDate
items.description: The date and time when the device was manufactured.

items.name: expirationDate
items.description: The date and time beyond which this device is no longer valid or should not be used (if applicable).

items.name: lotNumber
items.description: Lot number assigned by the manufacturer.

items.name: serialNumber
items.description: The serial number assigned by the organization when the device was manufactured.

items.name: deviceName
items.description: This represents the manufacturer's name of the device as provided by the device, from a UDI label, or by a person describing the Device.  This typically would be used when a person provides the name(s) or when the device represents one of the names available from DeviceDefinition.

items.name: modelNumber
items.description: The model number for the device.

items.name: partNumber
items.description: The part number of the device.

items.name: type
items.description: The kind or type of device.

items.name: specialization
items.description: The capabilities supported on a  device, the standards to which the device conforms for a particular purpose, and used for the communication.

items.name: version
items.description: The actual design of the device or software version running on the device.

items.name: property
items.description: The actual configuration settings of a device as it actually operates, e.g., regulation status, time properties.

items.name: patient
items.description: Patient information, If the device is affixed to a person.

items.name: owner
items.description: An organization that is responsible for the provision and ongoing maintenance of the device.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details for an organization or a particular human that is responsible for the device.

items.name: location
items.description: The place where the device can be found.

items.name: url
items.description: A network address on which the device may be contacted directly.

items.name: note
items.description: Descriptive information, usage information or implantation information that is not captured in an existing element.

items.name: safety
items.description: Provides additional safety characteristics about a medical device.  For example devices containing latex.

items.name: parent
items.description: The parent device.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Unique instance identifiers assigned to a device by the software, manufacturers, other organizations or owners. For example: handle ID.

items.name: udiDeviceIdentifier
items.description: Unique device identifier (UDI) assigned to device label or package.  Note that the Device may include multiple udiCarriers as it either may include just the udiCarrier for the jurisdiction it is sold, or for multiple jurisdictions it could have been sold.

items.name: manufacturer[x]
items.description: A name of the manufacturer.

items.name: deviceName
items.description: A name given to the device to identify it.

items.name: modelNumber
items.description: The model number for the device.

items.name: type
items.description: What kind of device or device system this is.

items.name: specialization
items.description: The capabilities supported on a  device, the standards to which the device conforms for a particular purpose, and used for the communication.

items.name: version
items.description: The available versions of the device, e.g., software versions.

items.name: safety
items.description: Safety characteristics of the device.

items.name: shelfLifeStorage
items.description: Shelf Life and storage information.

items.name: physicalCharacteristics
items.description: Dimensions, color etc.

items.name: languageCode
items.description: Language code for the human-readable text strings produced by the device (all supported).

items.name: capability
items.description: Device capabilities.

items.name: property
items.description: The actual configuration settings of a device as it actually operates, e.g., regulation status, time properties.

items.name: owner
items.description: An organization that is responsible for the provision and ongoing maintenance of the device.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details for an organization or a particular human that is responsible for the device.

items.name: url
items.description: A network address on which the device may be contacted directly.

items.name: onlineInformation
items.description: Access to on-line information about the device.

items.name: note
items.description: Descriptive information, usage information or implantation information that is not captured in an existing element.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The quantity of the device present in the packaging (e.g. the number of devices present in a pack, or the number of devices in the same package of the medicinal product).

items.name: parentDevice
items.description: The parent device it can be part of.

items.name: material
items.description: A substance used to create the material(s) of which the device is made.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Unique instance identifiers assigned to a device by the device or gateway software, manufacturers, other organizations or owners. For example: handle ID.

items.name: type
items.description: Describes the type of the metric. For example: Heart Rate, PEEP Setting, etc.

items.name: unit
items.description: Describes the unit that an observed value determined for this metric will have. For example: Percent, Seconds, etc.

items.name: source
items.description: Describes the link to the  Device that this DeviceMetric belongs to and that contains administrative device information such as manufacturer, serial number, etc.

items.name: parent
items.description: Describes the link to the  Device that this DeviceMetric belongs to and that provide information about the location of this DeviceMetric in the containment structure of the parent Device. An example would be a Device that represents a Channel. This reference can be used by a client application to distinguish DeviceMetrics that have the same type, but should be interpreted based on their containment location.

items.name: operationalStatus
items.description: Indicates current operational state of the device. For example: On, Off, Standby, etc.

items.name: color
items.description: Describes the color representation for the metric. This is often used to aid clinicians to track and identify parameter types by color. In practice, consider a Patient Monitor that has ECG/HR and Pleth for example; the parameters are displayed in different characteristic colors, such as HR-blue, BP-green, and PR and SpO2- magenta.

items.name: category
items.description: Indicates the category of the observation generation process. A DeviceMetric can be for example a setting, measurement, or calculation.

items.name: measurementPeriod
items.description: Describes the measurement repetition time. This is not necessarily the same as the update period. The measurement repetition time can range from milliseconds up to hours. An example for a measurement repetition time in the range of milliseconds is the sampling rate of an ECG. An example for a measurement repetition time in the range of hours is a NIBP that is triggered automatically every hour. The update period may be different than the measurement repetition time, if the device does not update the published observed value with the same frequency as it was measured.

items.name: calibration
items.description: Describes the calibrations that have been performed or that are required to be performed.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this order by the orderer or by the receiver.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this DeviceRequest.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this DeviceRequest.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: Plan/proposal/order fulfilled by this request.

items.name: priorRequest
items.description: The request takes the place of the referenced completed or terminated request(s).

items.name: groupIdentifier
items.description: Composite request this is part of.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the request.

items.name: intent
items.description: Whether the request is a proposal, plan, an original order or a reflex order.

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the {{title}} should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: code[x]
items.description: The details of the device to be used.

items.name: parameter
items.description: Specific parameters for the ordered item.  For example, the prism value for lenses.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient who will use the device.

items.name: encounter
items.description: An encounter that provides additional context in which this request is made.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: The timing schedule for the use of the device. The Schedule data type allows many different expressions, for example. "Every 8 hours"; "Three times a day"; "1/2 an hour before breakfast for 10 days from 23-Dec 2011:"; "15 Oct 2013, 17 Oct 2013 and 1 Nov 2013".

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: When the request transitioned to being actionable.

items.name: requester
items.description: The individual who initiated the request and has responsibility for its activation.

items.name: performerType
items.description: Desired type of performer for doing the diagnostic testing.

items.name: performer
items.description: The desired performer for doing the diagnostic testing.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Reason or justification for the use of this device.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Reason or justification for the use of this device.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Insurance plans, coverage extensions, pre-authorizations and/or pre-determinations that may be required for delivering the requested service.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional clinical information about the patient that may influence the request fulfilment.  For example, this may include where on the subject's body the device will be used (i.e. the target site).

items.name: note
items.description: Details about this request that were not represented at all or sufficiently in one of the attributes provided in a class. These may include for example a comment, an instruction, or a note associated with the statement.

items.name: relevantHistory
items.description: Key events in the history of the request.

items.name: identifier
items.description: An external identifier for this statement such as an IRI.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A plan, proposal or order that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this DeviceUseStatement.

items.name: status
items.description: A code representing the patient or other source's judgment about the state of the device used that this statement is about.  Generally this will be active or completed.

items.name: subject

items.name: derivedFrom

items.name: timing[x]

items.name: recordedOn

items.name: source

items.name: device
items.description: The details of the device used.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Reason or justification for the use of the device.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource whose existence justifies this DeviceUseStatement.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Indicates the anotomic location on the subject's body where the device was used ( i.e. the target).

items.name: note
items.description: Details about the device statement that were not represented at all or sufficiently in one of the attributes provided in a class. These may include for example a comment, an instruction, or a note associated with the statement.

structure.name: DiagnosticReport
structure.description: The findings and interpretation of diagnostic  tests performed on patients, groups of patients, devices, and locations, and/or specimens derived from these. The report includes clinical context such as requesting and provider information, and some mix of atomic results, images, textual and coded interpretations, and formatted representation of diagnostic reports.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this report by the performer or other systems.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: Details concerning a service requested.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the diagnostic report.

items.name: category
items.description: A code that classifies the clinical discipline, department or diagnostic service that created the report (e.g. cardiology, biochemistry, hematology, MRI). This is used for searching, sorting and display purposes.

items.name: code
items.description: A code or name that describes this diagnostic report.

items.name: subject
items.description: The subject of the report. Usually, but not always, this is a patient. However, diagnostic services also perform analyses on specimens collected from a variety of other sources.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The healthcare event  (e.g. a patient and healthcare provider interaction) which this DiagnosticReport is about.

items.name: effective[x]
items.description: The time or time-period the observed values are related to. When the subject of the report is a patient, this is usually either the time of the procedure or of specimen collection(s), but very often the source of the date/time is not known, only the date/time itself.

items.name: issued
items.description: The date and time that this version of the report was made available to providers, typically after the report was reviewed and verified.

items.name: performer
items.description: The diagnostic service that is responsible for issuing the report.

items.name: resultsInterpreter
items.description: The practitioner or organization that is responsible for the report's conclusions and interpretations.

items.name: specimen
items.description: Details about the specimens on which this diagnostic report is based.

items.name: result
items.description: [Observations](observation.html)  that are part of this diagnostic report.

items.name: imagingStudy
items.description: One or more links to full details of any imaging performed during the diagnostic investigation. Typically, this is imaging performed by DICOM enabled modalities, but this is not required. A fully enabled PACS viewer can use this information to provide views of the source images.

items.name: media
items.description: A list of key images associated with this report. The images are generally created during the diagnostic process, and may be directly of the patient, or of treated specimens (i.e. slides of interest).

items.name: conclusion
items.description: Concise and clinically contextualized summary conclusion (interpretation/impression) of the diagnostic report.

items.name: conclusionCode
items.description: One or more codes that represent the summary conclusion (interpretation/impression) of the diagnostic report.

items.name: presentedForm
items.description: Rich text representation of the entire result as issued by the diagnostic service. Multiple formats are allowed but they SHALL be semantically equivalent.

items.name: masterIdentifier
items.description: A single identifier that uniquely identifies this manifest. Principally used to refer to the manifest in non-FHIR contexts.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Other identifiers associated with the document manifest, including version independent  identifiers.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this document manifest.

items.name: type
items.description: The code specifying the type of clinical activity that resulted in placing the associated content into the DocumentManifest.

items.name: subject
items.description: Who or what the set of documents is about. The documents can be about a person, (patient or healthcare practitioner), a device (i.e. machine) or even a group of subjects (such as a document about a herd of farm animals, or a set of patients that share a common exposure). If the documents cross more than one subject, then more than one subject is allowed here (unusual use case).

items.name: created
items.description: When the document manifest was created for submission to the server (not necessarily the same thing as the actual resource last modified time, since it may be modified, replicated, etc.).

items.name: author
items.description: Identifies who is the author of the manifest. Manifest author is not necessarly the author of the references included.

items.name: recipient
items.description: A patient, practitioner, or organization for which this set of documents is intended.

items.name: source
items.description: Identifies the source system, application, or software that produced the document manifest.

items.name: description
items.description: Human-readable description of the source document. This is sometimes known as the "title".

items.name: content
items.description: The list of Resources that consist of the parts of this manifest.

items.name: related
items.description: Related identifiers or resources associated with the DocumentManifest.

structure.name: DocumentReference
structure.description: A reference to a document of any kind for any purpose. Provides metadata about the document so that the document can be discovered and managed. The scope of a document is any seralized object with a mime-type, so includes formal patient centric documents (CDA), cliical notes, scanned paper, and non-patient specific documents like policy text.

items.name: masterIdentifier
items.description: Document identifier as assigned by the source of the document. This identifier is specific to this version of the document. This unique identifier may be used elsewhere to identify this version of the document.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Other identifiers associated with the document, including version independent identifiers.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this document reference.

items.name: docStatus
items.description: The status of the underlying document.

items.name: type
items.description: Specifies the particular kind of document referenced  (e.g. History and Physical, Discharge Summary, Progress Note). This usually equates to the purpose of making the document referenced.

items.name: category
items.description: A categorization for the type of document referenced - helps for indexing and searching. This may be implied by or derived from the code specified in the DocumentReference.type.

items.name: subject
items.description: Who or what the document is about. The document can be about a person, (patient or healthcare practitioner), a device (e.g. a machine) or even a group of subjects (such as a document about a herd of farm animals, or a set of patients that share a common exposure).

items.name: date
items.description: When the document reference was created.

items.name: author
items.description: Identifies who is responsible for adding the information to the document.

items.name: authenticator
items.description: Which person or organization authenticates that this document is valid.

items.name: custodian
items.description: Identifies the organization or group who is responsible for ongoing maintenance of and access to the document.

items.name: relatesTo
items.description: Relationships that this document has with other document references that already exist.

items.name: description
items.description: Human-readable description of the source document.

items.name: securityLabel
items.description: A set of Security-Tag codes specifying the level of privacy/security of the Document. Note that DocumentReference.meta.security contains the security labels of the "reference" to the document, while DocumentReference.securityLabel contains a snapshot of the security labels on the document the reference refers to.

items.name: content
items.description: The document and format referenced. There may be multiple content element repetitions, each with a different format.

items.name: context
items.description: The clinical context in which the document was prepared.

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this effect evidence synthesis when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this effect evidence synthesis is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the effect evidence synthesis is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this effect evidence synthesis when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the effect evidence synthesis when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the effect evidence synthesis author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the effect evidence synthesis. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the effect evidence synthesis.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this effect evidence synthesis. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the effect evidence synthesis was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the effect evidence synthesis changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the effect evidence synthesis.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the effect evidence synthesis from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: note
items.description: A human-readable string to clarify or explain concepts about the resource.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate effect evidence synthesis instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the effect evidence synthesis is intended to be used.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the effect evidence synthesis and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the effect evidence synthesis.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the effect evidence synthesis content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the EffectEvidenceSynthesis. Topics provide a high-level categorization grouping types of EffectEvidenceSynthesiss that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: synthesisType
items.description: Type of synthesis eg meta-analysis.

items.name: studyType
items.description: Type of study eg randomized trial.

items.name: population
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resource that defines the population for the research.

items.name: exposure
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resource that defines the exposure for the research.

items.name: exposureAlternative
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resource that defines the comparison exposure for the research.

items.name: outcome
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resomece that defines the outcome for the research.

items.name: sampleSize
items.description: A description of the size of the sample involved in the synthesis.

items.name: resultsByExposure
items.description: A description of the results for each exposure considered in the effect estimate.

items.name: effectEstimate
items.description: The estimated effect of the exposure variant.

items.name: certainty
items.description: A description of the certainty of the effect estimate.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifier(s) by which this encounter is known.

items.name: status
items.description: planned | arrived | triaged | in-progress | onleave | finished | cancelled +.

items.name: statusHistory
items.description: The status history permits the encounter resource to contain the status history without needing to read through the historical versions of the resource, or even have the server store them.

items.name: class
items.description: Concepts representing classification of patient encounter such as ambulatory (outpatient), inpatient, emergency, home health or others due to local variations.

items.name: classHistory
items.description: The class history permits the tracking of the encounters transitions without needing to go  through the resource history.  This would be used for a case where an admission starts of as an emergency encounter, then transitions into an inpatient scenario. Doing this and not restarting a new encounter ensures that any lab/diagnostic results can more easily follow the patient and not require re-processing and not get lost or cancelled during a kind of discharge from emergency to inpatient.

items.name: type
items.description: Specific type of encounter (e.g. e-mail consultation, surgical day-care, skilled nursing, rehabilitation).

items.name: serviceType
items.description: Broad categorization of the service that is to be provided (e.g. cardiology).

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates the urgency of the encounter.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient or group present at the encounter.

items.name: episodeOfCare
items.description: Where a specific encounter should be classified as a part of a specific episode(s) of care this field should be used. This association can facilitate grouping of related encounters together for a specific purpose, such as government reporting, issue tracking, association via a common problem.  The association is recorded on the encounter as these are typically created after the episode of care and grouped on entry rather than editing the episode of care to append another encounter to it (the episode of care could span years).

items.name: basedOn
items.description: The request this encounter satisfies (e.g. incoming referral or procedure request).

items.name: participant
items.description: The list of people responsible for providing the service.

items.name: appointment
items.description: The appointment that scheduled this encounter.

items.name: period
items.description: The start and end time of the encounter.

items.name: length
items.description: Quantity of time the encounter lasted. This excludes the time during leaves of absence.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Reason the encounter takes place, expressed as a code. For admissions, this can be used for a coded admission diagnosis.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Reason the encounter takes place, expressed as a code. For admissions, this can be used for a coded admission diagnosis.

items.name: diagnosis
items.description: The list of diagnosis relevant to this encounter.

items.name: account
items.description: The set of accounts that may be used for billing for this Encounter.

items.name: hospitalization
items.description: Details about the admission to a healthcare service.

items.name: location
items.description: List of locations where  the patient has been during this encounter.

items.name: serviceProvider
items.description: The organization that is primarily responsible for this Encounter's services. This MAY be the same as the organization on the Patient record, however it could be different, such as if the actor performing the services was from an external organization (which may be billed seperately) for an external consultation.  Refer to the example bundle showing an abbreviated set of Encounters for a colonoscopy.

items.name: partOf
items.description: Another Encounter of which this encounter is a part of (administratively or in time).

structure.name: Endpoint
structure.description: The technical details of an endpoint that can be used for electronic services, such as for web services providing XDS.b or a REST endpoint for another FHIR server. This may include any security context information.

items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this event definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: subtitle

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this event definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this event definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the event definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the event definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. items.name: jurisdiction

items.name: purpose

items.name: usage

items.description: The period during which the event definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: reviewer

items.name: endorser

items.name: type

items.name: characteristic

items.name: purpose

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this explanation of benefit.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: The category of claim, e.g. oral, pharmacy, vision, institutional, professional.

items.name: subType
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: use
items.description: A code to indicate whether the nature of the request is: to request adjudication of products and services previously rendered; or requesting authorization and adjudication for provision in the future; or requesting the non-binding adjudication of the listed products and services which could be provided in the future.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party to whom the professional services and/or products have been supplied or are being considered and for whom actual for forecast reimbursement is sought.

items.name: billablePeriod
items.description: The period for which charges are being submitted.

items.name: created
items.description: The date this resource was created.

items.name: enterer
items.description: Individual who created the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The party responsible for authorization, adjudication and reimbursement.

items.name: provider
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: priority
items.description: The provider-required urgency of processing the request. Typical values include: stat, routine deferred.

items.name: fundsReserveRequested
items.description: A code to indicate whether and for whom funds are to be reserved for future claims.

items.name: fundsReserve
items.description: A code, used only on a response to a preauthorization, to indicate whether the benefits payable have been reserved and for whom.

items.name: related
items.description: Other claims which are related to this claim such as prior submissions or claims for related services or for the same event.

items.name: prescription
items.description: Prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy, device or vision products.

items.name: originalPrescription
items.description: Original prescription which has been superseded by this prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy services, medications or products.

items.name: payee
items.description: The party to be reimbursed for cost of the products and services according to the terms of the policy.

items.name: referral
items.description: A reference to a referral resource.

items.name: facility
items.description: Facility where the services were provided.

items.name: claim
items.description: The business identifier for the instance of the adjudication request: claim predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: claimResponse
items.description: The business identifier for the instance of the adjudication response: claim, predetermination or preauthorization response.

items.name: outcome
items.description: The outcome of the claim, predetermination, or preauthorization processing.

items.name: disposition
items.description: A human readable description of the status of the adjudication.

items.name: preAuthRef
items.description: Reference from the Insurer which is used in later communications which refers to this adjudication.

items.name: preAuthRefPeriod
items.description: The timeframe during which the supplied preauthorization reference may be quoted on claims to obtain the adjudication as provided.

items.name: careTeam
items.description: The members of the team who provided the products and services.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional information codes regarding exceptions, special considerations, the condition, situation, prior or concurrent issues.

items.name: diagnosis
items.description: Information about diagnoses relevant to the claim items.

items.name: procedure
items.description: Procedures performed on the patient relevant to the billing items with the claim.

items.name: precedence
items.description: This indicates the relative order of a series of EOBs related to different coverages for the same suite of services.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: accident
items.description: Details of a accident which resulted in injuries which required the products and services listed in the claim.

items.name: item
items.description: A claim line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of details which can also be a simple items or groups of sub-details.

items.name: addItem
items.description: The first-tier service adjudications for payor added product or service lines.

items.name: adjudication
items.description: The adjudication results which are presented at the header level rather than at the line-item or add-item levels.

items.name: total
items.description: Categorized monetary totals for the adjudication.

items.name: payment
items.description: Payment details for the adjudication of the claim.

items.name: formCode
items.description: A code for the form to be used for printing the content.

items.name: form
items.description: The actual form, by reference or inclusion, for printing the content or an EOB.

items.name: processNote
items.description: A note that describes or explains adjudication results in a human readable form.

items.name: benefitPeriod
items.description: The term of the benefits documented in this response.

items.name: benefitBalance
items.description: Balance by Benefit Category.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this family member history by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this FamilyMemberHistory.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this FamilyMemberHistory.

items.name: status
items.description: A code specifying the status of the record of the family history of a specific family member.

items.name: dataAbsentReason
items.description: Describes why the family member's history is not available.

items.name: patient
items.description: The person who this history concerns.

items.name: date
items.description: The date (and possibly time) when the family member history was recorded or last updated.

items.name: name
items.description: This will either be a name or a description; e.g. "Aunt Susan", "my cousin with the red hair".

items.name: relationship
items.description: The type of relationship this person has to the patient (father, mother, brother etc.).

items.name: sex
items.description: The birth sex of the family member.

items.name: born[x]
items.description: The actual or approximate date of birth of the relative.

items.name: age[x]
items.description: The age of the relative at the time the family member history is recorded.

items.name: estimatedAge
items.description: If true, indicates that the age value specified is an estimated value.

items.name: deceased[x]
items.description: Deceased flag or the actual or approximate age of the relative at the time of death for the family member history record.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes why the family member history occurred in coded or textual form.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates a Condition, Observation, AllergyIntolerance, or QuestionnaireResponse that justifies this family member history event.

items.name: note
items.description: This property allows a non condition-specific note to the made about the related person. Ideally, the note would be in the condition property, but this is not always possible.

items.name: condition
items.description: The significant Conditions (or condition) that the family member had. This is a repeating section to allow a system to represent more than one condition per resource, though there is nothing stopping multiple resources - one per condition.

structure.name: Flag
structure.description: Prospective warnings of potential issues when providing care to the patient.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this flag by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: status
items.description: Supports basic workflow.

items.name: category
items.description: Allows a flag to be divided into different categories like clinical, administrative etc. Intended to be used as a means of filtering which flags are displayed to particular user or in a given context.

items.name: code
items.description: The coded value or textual component of the flag to display to the user.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient, location, group, organization, or practitioner etc. this is about record this flag is associated with.

items.name: period
items.description: The period of time from the activation of the flag to inactivation of the flag. If the flag is active, the end of the period should be unspecified.

items.name: encounter
items.description: This alert is only relevant during the encounter.

items.name: author
items.description: The person, organization or device that created the flag.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this goal by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: lifecycleStatus
items.description: The state of the goal throughout its lifecycle.

items.name: achievementStatus
items.description: Describes the progression, or lack thereof, towards the goal against the target.

items.name: category
items.description: Indicates a category the goal falls within.

items.name: priority
items.description: Identifies the mutually agreed level of importance associated with reaching/sustaining the goal.

items.name: description
items.description: Human-readable and/or coded description of a specific desired objective of care, such as "control blood pressure" or "negotiate an obstacle course" or "dance with child at wedding".

items.name: subject
items.description: Identifies the patient, group or organization for whom the goal is being established.

items.name: start[x]
items.description: The date or event after which the goal should begin being pursued.

items.name: target
items.description: Indicates what should be done by when.

items.name: statusDate
items.description: Identifies when the current status.  I.e. When initially created, when achieved, when cancelled, etc.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current status.

items.name: expressedBy
items.description: Indicates whose goal this is - patient goal, practitioner goal, etc.

items.name: addresses
items.description: The identified conditions and other health record elements that are intended to be addressed by the goal.

items.name: note
items.description: Any comments related to the goal.

items.name: outcomeCode
items.description: Identifies the change (or lack of change) at the point when the status of the goal is assessed.

items.name: outcomeReference
items.description: Details of what's changed (or not changed).

items.name: link
items.description: Links this graph makes rules about.

structure.name: Group
structure.description: Represents a defined collection of entities that may be discussed or acted upon collectively but which are not expected to act collectively, and are not formally or legally recognized; i.e. a collection of entities that isn't an Organization.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique business identifier for this group.

items.name: active
items.description: Indicates whether the record for the group is available for use or is merely being retained for historical purposes.

items.name: type
items.description: Identifies the broad classification of the kind of resources the group includes.

items.name: actual
items.description: If true, indicates that the resource refers to a specific group of real individuals.  If false, the group defines a set of intended individuals.

items.name: code
items.description: Provides a specific type of resource the group includes; e.g. "cow", "syringe", etc.

items.name: name
items.description: A label assigned to the group for human identification and communication.

items.name: quantity
items.description: A count of the number of resource instances that are part of the group.

items.name: managingEntity
items.description: Entity responsible for defining and maintaining Group characteristics and/or registered members.

items.name: characteristic
items.description: Identifies traits whose presence r absence is shared by members of the group.

items.name: member
items.description: Identifies the resource instances that are members of the group.

structure.name: GuidanceResponse
structure.description: A guidance response is the formal response to a guidance request, including any output parameters returned by the evaluation, as well as the description of any proposed actions to be taken.

items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta

items.name: requestIdentifier
items.description: The identifier of the request associated with this response. If an identifier was given as part of the request, it will be reproduced here to enable the requester to more easily identify the response in a multi-request scenario.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Allows a service to provide  unique, business identifiers for the response.

items.name: module[x]
items.description: An identifier, CodeableConcept or canonical reference to the guidance that was requested.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the response. If the evaluation is completed successfully, the status will indicate success. However, in order to complete the evaluation, the engine may require more information. In this case, the status will be data-required, and the response will contain a description of the additional required information. If the evaluation completed successfully, but the engine determines that a potentially more accurate response could be provided if more data was available, the status will be data-requested, and the response will contain a description of the additional requested information.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient for which the request was processed.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The encounter during which this response was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: occurrenceDateTime
items.description: Indicates when the guidance response was processed.

items.name: performer
items.description: Provides a reference to the device that performed the guidance.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes the reason for the guidance response in coded or textual form.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates the reason the request was initiated. This is typically provided as a parameter to the evaluation and echoed by the service, although for some use cases, such as subscription- or event-based scenarios, it may provide an indication of the cause for the response.

items.name: note
items.description: Provides a mechanism to communicate additional information about the response.

items.name: evaluationMessage
items.description: Messages resulting from the evaluation of the artifact or artifacts. As part of evaluating the request, the engine may produce informational or warning messages. These messages will be provided by this element.

items.name: outputParameters
items.description: The output parameters of the evaluation, if any. Many modules will result in the return of specific resources such as procedure or communication requests that are returned as part of the operation result. However, modules may define specific outputs that would be returned as the result of the evaluation, and these would be returned in this element.

items.name: result
items.description: The actions, if any, produced by the evaluation of the artifact.

items.name: dataRequirement
items.description: If the evaluation could not be completed due to lack of information, or additional information would potentially result in a more accurate response, this element will a description of the data required in order to proceed with the evaluation. A subsequent request to the service should include this data.

structure.name: HealthcareService
structure.description: The details of a healthcare service available at a location.

items.name: meta

items.name: identifier
items.description: External identifiers for this item.

items.name: active
items.description: This flag is used to mark the record to not be used. This is not used when a center is closed for maintenance, or for holidays, the notAvailable period is to be used for this.

items.name: providedBy
items.description: The organization that provides this healthcare service.

items.name: category
items.description: Identifies the broad category of service being performed or delivered.

items.name: type
items.description: The specific type of service that may be delivered or performed.

items.name: specialty
items.description: Collection of specialties handled by the service site. This is more of a medical term.

items.name: location
items.description: The location(s) where this healthcare service may be provided.

items.name: name
items.description: Further description of the service as it would be presented to a consumer while searching.

items.name: comment
items.description: Any additional description of the service and/or any specific issues not covered by the other attributes, which can be displayed as further detail under the serviceName.

items.name: extraDetails
items.description: Extra details about the service that can't be placed in the other fields.

items.name: photo
items.description: If there is a photo/symbol associated with this HealthcareService, it may be included here to facilitate quick identification of the service in a list.

items.name: telecom
items.description: List of contacts related to this specific healthcare service.

items.name: coverageArea
items.description: The location(s) that this service is available to (not where the service is provided).

items.name: serviceProvisionCode
items.description: The code(s) that detail the conditions under which the healthcare service is available/offered.

items.name: eligibility
items.description: Does this service have specific eligibility requirements that need to be met in order to use the service?

items.name: program
items.description: Programs that this service is applicable to.

items.name: characteristic
items.description: Collection of characteristics (attributes).

items.name: communication
items.description: Some services are specifically made available in multiple languages, this property permits a directory to declare the languages this is offered in. Typically this is only provided where a service operates in communities with mixed languages used.

items.name: referralMethod
items.description: Ways that the service accepts referrals, if this is not provided then it is implied that no referral is required.

items.name: appointmentRequired
items.description: Indicates whether or not a prospective consumer will require an appointment for a particular service at a site to be provided by the Organization. Indicates if an appointment is required for access to this service.

items.name: availableTime
items.description: A collection of times that the Service Site is available.

items.name: notAvailable
items.description: The HealthcareService is not available during this period of time due to the provided reason.

items.name: availabilityExceptions
items.description: A description of site availability exceptions, e.g. public holiday availability. Succinctly describing all possible exceptions to normal site availability as details in the available Times and not available Times.

items.name: endpoint
items.description: Technical endpoints providing access to services operated for the specific healthcare services defined at this resource.

structure.name: ImagingStudy
structure.description: Representation of the content produced in a DICOM imaging study. A study comprises a set of series, each of which includes a set of Service-Object Pair Instances (SOP Instances - images or other data) acquired or produced in a common context.  A series is of only one modality (e.g. X-ray, CT, MR, ultrasound), but a study may have multiple series of different modalities.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers for the ImagingStudy such as DICOM Study Instance UID, and Accession Number.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the ImagingStudy.

items.name: modality
items.description: A list of all the series.modality values that are actual acquisition modalities, i.e. those in the DICOM Context Group 29 (value set OID 1.2.840.10008.6.1.19).

items.name: subject
items.description: The subject, typically a patient, of the imaging study.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The healthcare event (e.g. a patient and healthcare provider interaction) during which this ImagingStudy is made.

items.name: started
items.description: Date and time the study started.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A list of the diagnostic requests that resulted in this imaging study being performed.

items.name: referrer
items.description: The requesting/referring physician.

items.name: interpreter
items.description: Who read the study and interpreted the images or other content.

items.name: endpoint
items.description: The network service providing access (e.g., query, view, or retrieval) for the study. See implementation notes for information about using DICOM endpoints. A study-level endpoint applies to each series in the study, unless overridden by a series-level endpoint with the same Endpoint.connectionType.

items.name: numberOfSeries
items.description: Number of Series in the Study. This value given may be larger than the number of series elements this Resource contains due to resource availability, security, or other factors. This element should be present if any series elements are present.

items.name: numberOfInstances
items.description: Number of SOP Instances in Study. This value given may be larger than the number of instance elements this resource contains due to resource availability, security, or other factors. This element should be present if any instance elements are present.

items.name: procedureReference
items.description: The procedure which this ImagingStudy was part of.

items.name: procedureCode
items.description: The code for the performed procedure type.

items.name: location
items.description: The principal physical location where the ImagingStudy was performed.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Description of clinical condition indicating why the ImagingStudy was requested.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource whose existence justifies this Study.

items.name: note
items.description: Per the recommended DICOM mapping, this element is derived from the Study Description attribute (0008,1030). Observations or findings about the imaging study should be recorded in another resource, e.g. Observation, and not in this element.

items.name: description
items.description: The Imaging Manager description of the study. Institution-generated description or classification of the Study (component) performed.

items.name: series
items.description: Each study has one or more series of images or other content.
