items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this immunization record.

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates the current status of the immunization event.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Indicates the reason the immunization event was not performed.

items.name: vaccineCode
items.description: Vaccine that was administered or was to be administered.

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient who either received or did not receive the immunization.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The visit or admission or other contact between patient and health care provider the immunization was performed as part of.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: Date vaccine administered or was to be administered.

items.name: recorded
items.description: The date the occurrence of the immunization was first captured in the record - potentially significantly after the occurrence of the event.

items.name: primarySource
items.description: An indication that the content of the record is based on information from the person who administered the vaccine. This reflects the context under which the data was originally recorded.

items.name: reportOrigin
items.description: The source of the data when the report of the immunization event is not based on information from the person who administered the vaccine.

items.name: location
items.description: The service delivery location where the vaccine administration occurred.

items.name: manufacturer
items.description: Name of vaccine manufacturer.

items.name: lotNumber
items.description: Lot number of the  vaccine product.

items.name: expirationDate
items.description: Date vaccine batch expires.

items.name: site
items.description: Body site where vaccine was administered.

items.name: route
items.description: The path by which the vaccine product is taken into the body.

items.name: doseQuantity
items.description: The quantity of vaccine product that was administered.

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates who performed the immunization event.

items.name: note
items.description: Extra information about the immunization that is not conveyed by the other attributes.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Reasons why the vaccine was administered.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Condition, Observation or DiagnosticReport that supports why the immunization was administered.

items.name: isSubpotent
items.description: Indication if a dose is considered to be subpotent. By default, a dose should be considered to be potent.

items.name: subpotentReason
items.description: Reason why a dose is considered to be subpotent.

items.name: education
items.description: Educational material presented to the patient (or guardian) at the time of vaccine administration.

items.name: programEligibility
items.description: Indicates a patient's eligibility for a funding program.

items.name: fundingSource
items.description: Indicates the source of the vaccine actually administered. This may be different than the patient eligibility (e.g. the patient may be eligible for a publically purchased vaccine but due to inventory issues, vaccine purchased with private funds was actually administered).

items.name: reaction
items.description: Categorical data indicating that an adverse event is associated in time to an immunization.

items.name: protocolApplied
items.description: The protocol (set of recommendations) being followed by the provider who administered the dose.

structure.name: ImmunizationEvaluation
structure.description: Describes a comparison of an immunization event against published recommendations to determine if the administration is "valid" in relation to those  recommendations.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this immunization evaluation record.

items.name: status
items.description: Indicates the current status of the evaluation of the vaccination administration event.

items.name: patient
items.description: The individual for whom the evaluation is being done.

items.name: date
items.description: The date the evaluation of the vaccine administration event was performed.

items.name: authority
items.description: Indicates the authority who published the protocol (e.g. ACIP).

items.name: targetDisease
items.description: The vaccine preventable disease the dose is being evaluated against.

items.name: immunizationEvent
items.description: The vaccine administration event being evaluated.

items.name: doseStatus
items.description: Indicates if the dose is valid or not valid with respect to the published recommendations.

items.name: doseStatusReason
items.description: Provides an explanation as to why the vaccine administration event is valid or not relative to the published recommendations.

items.name: description
items.description: Additional information about the evaluation.

items.name: series
items.description: One possible path to achieve presumed immunity against a disease - within the context of an authority.

items.name: doseNumber[x]
items.description: Nominal position in a series.

items.name: seriesDoses[x]
items.description: The recommended number of doses to achieve immunity.

structure.name: ImmunizationRecommendation
structure.description: A patient's point-in-time set of recommendations (i.e. forecasting) according to a published schedule with optional supporting justification.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this particular recommendation record.

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient the recommendation(s) are for.

items.name: date
items.description: The date the immunization recommendation(s) were created.

items.name: authority
items.description: Indicates the authority who published the protocol (e.g. ACIP).

This resource is used to gather all the parts of an implementation guide into a logical whole and to publish a computable definition of all the parts.

The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. items.name: status

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this health insurance product which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the health insurance product.

items.name: type
items.description: The kind of health insurance product.

items.name: name
items.description: Official name of the health insurance product as designated by the owner.

items.name: alias
items.description: A list of alternate names that the product is known as, or was known as in the past.

items.name: period
items.description: The period of time that the health insurance product is available.

items.name: ownedBy
items.description: The entity that is providing  the health insurance product and underwriting the risk.  This is typically an insurance carriers, other third-party payers, or health plan sponsors comonly referred to as 'payers'.

items.name: administeredBy
items.description: An organization which administer other services such as underwriting, customer service and/or claims processing on behalf of the health insurance product owner.

items.name: coverageArea
items.description: The geographic region in which a health insurance product's benefits apply.

items.name: contact
items.description: The contact for the health insurance product for a certain purpose.

items.name: endpoint
items.description: The technical endpoints providing access to services operated for the health insurance product.

items.name: network
items.description: Reference to the network included in the health insurance product.

items.name: coverage
items.description: Details about the coverage offered by the insurance product.

items.name: plan
items.description: Details about an insurance plan.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifier of this Invoice, often used for reference in correspondence about this invoice or for tracking of payments.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the Invoice.

items.name: cancelledReason
items.description: In case of Invoice cancellation a reason must be given (entered in error, superseded by corrected invoice etc.).

items.name: type
items.description: Type of Invoice depending on domain, realm an usage (e.g. internal/external, dental, preliminary).

items.name: subject
items.description: The individual or set of individuals receiving the goods and services billed in this invoice.

items.name: recipient
items.description: The individual or Organization responsible for balancing of this invoice.

items.name: date
items.description: Date/time(s) of when this Invoice was posted.

items.name: participant
items.description: Indicates who or what performed or participated in the charged service.

items.name: issuer
items.description: The organizationissuing the Invoice.

items.name: account
items.description: Account which is supposed to be balanced with this Invoice.

items.name: lineItem
items.description: Each line item represents one charge for goods and services rendered. Details such as date, code and amount are found in the referenced ChargeItem resource.

items.name: totalPriceComponent
items.description: The total amount for the Invoice may be calculated as the sum of the line items with surcharges/deductions that apply in certain conditions.  The priceComponent element can be used to offer transparency to the recipient of the Invoice of how the total price was calculated.

items.name: totalNet
items.description: Invoice total , taxes excluded.

items.name: totalGross
items.description: Invoice total, tax included.

items.name: paymentTerms
items.description: Payment details such as banking details, period of payment, deductibles, methods of payment.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the invoice by the issuer, subject, or other participants.

items.description: Name of the location as used by humans. Does not need to be unique.

items.name: alias
items.description: A list of alternate names that the location is known as, or was known as, in the past.

items.name: description
items.description: Description of the Location, which helps in finding or referencing the place.

items.name: mode
items.description: Indicates whether a resource instance represents a specific location or a class of locations.

items.name: type
items.description: Indicates the type of function performed at the location.

items.name: telecom
items.description: The contact details of communication devices available at the location. This can include phone numbers, fax numbers, mobile numbers, email addresses and web sites.

items.name: address
items.description: Physical location.

items.name: physicalType
items.description: Physical form of the location, e.g. building, room, vehicle, road.

items.name: position
items.description: The absolute geographic location of the Location, expressed using the WGS84 datum (This is the same co-ordinate system used in KML).

items.name: managingOrganization
items.description: The organization responsible for the provisioning and upkeep of the location.

items.name: partOf
items.description: Another Location of which this Location is physically a part of.

items.name: hoursOfOperation
items.description: What days/times during a week is this location usually open.

items.name: availabilityExceptions
items.description: A description of when the locations opening ours are different to normal, e.g. public holiday availability. Succinctly describing all possible exceptions to normal site availability as detailed in the opening hours Times.

items.name: endpoint
items.description: Technical endpoints providing access to services operated for the location.

structure.name: Measure
structure.description: The Measure resource provides the definition of a quality measure.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.description: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this measure when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this measure is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the measure is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this measure when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the measure when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the measure author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. To provide a version consistent with the Decision Support Service specification, use the format Major.Minor.Revision (e.g. 1.0.0). For more information on versioning knowledge assets, refer to the Decision Support Service specification. Note that a version is required for non-experimental active artifacts.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the measure. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the measure.

items.name: subtitle
items.description: An explanatory or alternate title for the measure giving additional information about its content.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this measure. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this measure is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: subject[x]
items.description: The intended subjects for the measure. If this element is not provided, a Patient subject is assumed, but the subject of the measure can be anything.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the measure was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the measure changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the measure.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the measure from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate measure instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the measure is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this measure is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: usage
items.description: A detailed description, from a clinical perspective, of how the measure is used.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the measure and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the measure.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the measure content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the measure. Topics provide a high-level categorization grouping types of measures that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: library
items.description: A reference to a Library resource containing the formal logic used by the measure.

items.name: disclaimer
items.description: Notices and disclaimers regarding the use of the measure or related to intellectual property (such as code systems) referenced by the measure.

items.name: scoring
items.description: Indicates how the calculation is performed for the measure, including proportion, ratio, continuous-variable, and cohort. The value set is extensible, allowing additional measure scoring types to be represented.

items.name: compositeScoring
items.description: If this is a composite measure, the scoring method used to combine the component measures to determine the composite score.

items.name: type
items.description: Indicates whether the measure is used to examine a process, an outcome over time, a patient-reported outcome, or a structure measure such as utilization.

items.name: riskAdjustment
items.description: A description of the risk adjustment factors that may impact the resulting score for the measure and how they may be accounted for when computing and reporting measure results.

items.name: rateAggregation
items.description: Describes how to combine the information calculated, based on logic in each of several populations, into one summarized result.

items.name: rationale
items.description: Provides a succinct statement of the need for the measure. Usually includes statements pertaining to importance criterion: impact, gap in care, and evidence.

items.name: clinicalRecommendationStatement
items.description: Provides a summary of relevant clinical guidelines or other clinical recommendations supporting the measure.

items.name: improvementNotation
items.description: Information on whether an increase or decrease in score is the preferred result (e.g., a higher score indicates better quality OR a lower score indicates better quality OR quality is within a range).

items.name: definition
items.description: Provides a description of an individual term used within the measure.

items.name: guidance
items.description: Additional guidance for the measure including how it can be used in a clinical context, and the intent of the measure.

items.name: group
items.description: A group of population criteria for the measure.

items.name: supplementalData
items.description: The supplemental data criteria for the measure report, specified as either the name of a valid CQL expression within a referenced library, or a valid FHIR Resource Path.

structure.name: MeasureReport
structure.description: The MeasureReport resource contains the results of the calculation of a measure; and optionally a reference to the resources involved in that calculation.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules

items.name: language

items.name: text

The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this MeasureReport when it is represented in other formats or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: status
items.description: The MeasureReport status. No data will be available until the MeasureReport status is complete.

items.name: type
items.description: The type of measure report. This may be an individual report, which provides the score for the measure for an individual member of the population; a subject-listing, which returns the list of members that meet the various criteria in the measure; a summary report, which returns a population count for each of the criteria in the measure; or a data-collection, which enables the MeasureReport to be used to exchange the data-of-interest for a quality measure.

items.name: measure
items.description: A reference to the Measure that was calculated to produce this report.

items.name: subject
items.description: Optional subject identifying the individual or individuals the report is for.

items.name: date
items.description: The date this measure report was generated.

items.name: reporter
items.description: The individual, location, or organization that is reporting the data.

items.name: period
items.description: The reporting period for which the report was calculated.

items.name: improvementNotation
items.description: Whether improvement in the measure is noted by an increase or decrease in the measure score.

items.name: group
items.description: The results of the calculation, one for each population group in the measure.

items.name: evaluatedResource
items.description: A reference to a Bundle containing the Resources that were used in the calculation of this measure.

structure.name: Media
structure.description: A photo, video, or audio recording acquired or used in healthcare. The actual content may be inline or provided by direct reference.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers associated with the image - these may include identifiers for the image itself, identifiers for the context of its collection (e.g. series ids) and context ids such as accession numbers or other workflow identifiers.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A procedure that is fulfilled in whole or in part by the creation of this media.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger event of which this particular event is a component or step.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the {{title}}.

items.name: type
items.description: A code that classifies whether the media is an image, video or audio recording or some other media category.

items.name: modality
items.description: Details of the type of the media - usually, how it was acquired (what type of device). If images sourced from a DICOM system, are wrapped in a Media resource, then this is the modality.

items.name: view
items.description: The name of the imaging view e.g. Lateral or Antero-posterior (AP).

items.name: subject
items.description: Who/What this Media is a record of.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The encounter that establishes the context for this media.

items.name: created[x]
items.description: The date and time(s) at which the media was collected.

items.name: issued
items.description: The date and time this version of the media was made available to providers, typically after having been reviewed.

items.name: operator
items.description: The person who administered the collection of the image.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes why the event occurred in coded or textual form.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Indicates the site on the subject's body where the observation was made (i.e. the target site).

items.name: deviceName
items.description: The name of the device / manufacturer of the device  that was used to make the recording.

items.name: device
items.description: The device used to collect the media.

items.name: height
items.description: Height of the image in pixels (photo/video).

items.name: width
items.description: Width of the image in pixels (photo/video).

items.name: frames
items.description: The number of frames in a photo. This is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slices in a single image, or an animated gif. If there is more than one frame, this SHALL have a value in order to alert interface software that a multi-frame capable rendering widget is required.

items.name: duration
items.description: The duration of the recording in seconds - for audio and video.

items.name: content
items.description: The actual content of the media - inline or by direct reference to the media source file.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the media by the performer, subject or other participants.

structure.name: Medication
structure.description: This resource is primarily used for the identification and definition of a medication for the purposes of prescribing, dispensing, and administering a medication as well as for making statements about medication use.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifier for this medication.

items.name: code
items.description: A code (or set of codes) that specify this medication, or a textual description if no code is available. Usage note: This could be a standard medication code such as a code from RxNorm, SNOMED CT, IDMP etc. It could also be a national or local formulary code, optionally with translations to other code systems.

items.name: status
items.description: A code to indicate if the medication is in active use.

items.name: manufacturer
items.description: Describes the details of the manufacturer of the medication product.  This is not intended to represent the distributor of a medication product.

items.name: form
items.description: Describes the form of the item.  Powder; tablets; capsule.

items.name: amount
items.description: Specific amount of the drug in the packaged product.  For example, when specifying a product that has the same strength (For example, Insulin glargine 100 unit per mL solution for injection), this attribute provides additional clarification of the package amount (For example, 3 mL, 10mL, etc.).

items.name: ingredient
items.description: Identifies a particular constituent of interest in the product.

items.name: batch
items.description: Information that only applies to packages (not products).

structure.name: MedicationAdministration
structure.description: Describes the event of a patient consuming or otherwise being administered a medication.  This may be as simple as swallowing a tablet or it may be a long running infusion.  Related resources tie this event to the authorizing prescription, and the specific encounter between patient and health care practitioner.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers associated with this Medication Administration that are defined by business processes and/or used to refer to it when a direct URL reference to the resource itself is not appropriate. They are business identifiers assigned to this resource by the performer or other systems and remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: instantiates
items.description: A protocol, guideline, orderset, or other definition that was adhered to in whole or in part by this event.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger event of which this particular event is a component or step.

items.name: status
items.description: Will generally be set to show that the administration has been completed.  For some long running administrations such as infusions, it is possible for an administration to be started but not completed or it may be paused while some other process is under way.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: A code indicating why the administration was not performed.

items.name: category
items.description: Indicates where the medication is expected to be consumed or administered.

items.name: medication[x]
items.description: Identifies the medication that was administered. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the medication or a simple attribute carrying a code that identifies the medication from a known list of medications.

items.name: subject
items.description: The person or animal or group receiving the medication.

items.name: context
items.description: The visit, admission, or other contact between patient and health care provider during which the medication administration was performed.

items.name: supportingInformation
items.description: Additional information (for example, patient height and weight) that supports the administration of the medication.

items.name: effective[x]
items.description: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place, when the 'notGiven' attribute is true). For many administrations, such as swallowing a tablet the use of dateTime is more appropriate.

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates who or what performed the medication administration and how they were involved.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: A code indicating why the medication was given.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Condition or observation that supports why the medication was administered.

items.name: request
items.description: The original request, instruction or authority to perform the administration.

items.name: device
items.description: The device used in administering the medication to the patient.  For example, a particular infusion pump.

items.name: note
items.description: Extra information about the medication administration that is not conveyed by the other attributes.

items.name: dosage
items.description: Describes the medication dosage information details e.g. dose, rate, site, route, etc.

items.name: eventHistory
items.description: A summary of the events of interest that have occurred, such as when the administration was verified.

structure.name: MedicationDispense
structure.description: Indicates that a medication product is to be or has been dispensed for a named person/patient.  This includes a description of the medication product (supply) provided and the instructions for administering the medication.  The medication dispense is the result of a pharmacy system responding to a medication order.

items.name: status

items.name: statusReason[x]
items.description: Indicates the reason why a dispense was not performed.

items.name: category
items.description: Indicates the type of medication dispense (for example, where the medication is expected to be consumed or administered (i.e. inpatient or outpatient)).

items.name: medication[x]
items.description: Identifies the medication being administered. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the medication or a simple attribute carrying a code that identifies the medication from a known list of medications.

items.name: subject
items.description: A link to a resource representing the person or the group to whom the medication will be given.

items.name: context
items.description: The encounter or episode of care that establishes the context for this event.

items.name: supportingInformation
items.description: Additional information that supports the medication being dispensed.

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates who or what performed the event.

items.name: location
items.description: The principal physical location where the dispense was performed.

items.name: authorizingPrescription
items.description: Indicates the medication order that is being dispensed against.

items.name: type
items.description: Indicates the type of dispensing event that is performed. For example, Trial Fill, Completion of Trial, Partial Fill, Emergency Fill, Samples, etc.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The amount of medication that has been dispensed. Includes unit of measure.

items.name: daysSupply
items.description: The amount of medication expressed as a timing amount.

items.name: whenPrepared
items.description: The time when the dispensed product was packaged and reviewed.

items.name: whenHandedOver
items.description: The time the dispensed product was provided to the patient or their representative.

items.name: destination
items.description: Identification of the facility/location where the medication was shipped to, as part of the dispense event.

items.name: receiver
items.description: Identifies the person who picked up the medication.  This will usually be a patient or their caregiver, but some cases exist where it can be a healthcare professional.

items.name: note
items.description: Extra information about the dispense that could not be conveyed in the other attributes.

items.name: dosageInstruction
items.description: Indicates how the medication is to be used by the patient.

items.name: substitution
items.description: Indicates whether or not substitution was made as part of the dispense.  In some cases, substitution will be expected but does not happen, in other cases substitution is not expected but does happen.  This block explains what substitution did or did not happen and why.  If nothing is specified, substitution was not done.

items.name: detectedIssue
items.description: Indicates an actual or potential clinical issue with or between one or more active or proposed clinical actions for a patient; e.g. drug-drug interaction, duplicate therapy, dosage alert etc.

items.name: eventHistory
items.description: A summary of the events of interest that have occurred, such as when the dispense was verified.

items.name: code
items.description: A code that specifies this medication, or a textual description if no code is available. Usage note: This could be a standard medication code such as a code from RxNorm, SNOMED CT, IDMP etc. It could also be a national or local formulary code, optionally with translations to other code systems.

items.name: status
items.description: A code to indicate if the medication is in active use.  The status refers to the validity about the information of the medication and not to its medicinal properties.

items.name: manufacturer
items.description: Describes the details of the manufacturer of the medication product.  This is not intended to represent the distributor of a medication product.

items.name: doseForm
items.description: Describes the form of the item.  Powder; tablets; capsule.

items.name: amount
items.description: Specific amount of the drug in the packaged product.  For example, when specifying a product that has the same strength (For example, Insulin glargine 100 unit per mL solution for injection), this attribute provides additional clarification of the package amount (For example, 3 mL, 10mL, etc.).

items.name: synonym
items.description: Additional names for a medication, for example, the name(s) given to a medication in different countries.  For example, acetaminophen and paracetamol or salbutamol and albuterol.

items.name: relatedMedicationKnowledge
items.description: Associated or related knowledge about a medication.

items.name: associatedMedication
items.description: Associated or related medications.  For example, if the medication is a branded product (e.g. Crestor), this is the Therapeutic Moeity (e.g. Rosuvastatin) or if this is a generic medication (e.g. Rosuvastatin), this would link to a branded product (e.g. Crestor).

items.name: productType
items.description: Category of the medication or product (e.g. branded product, therapeutic moeity, generic product, innovator product, etc.).

items.name: monograph
items.description: Associated documentation about the medication.

items.name: ingredient
items.description: Identifies a particular constituent of interest in the product.

items.name: preparationInstruction
items.description: The instructions for preparing the medication.

items.name: intendedRoute
items.description: The intended or approved route of administration.

items.name: cost
items.description: The price of the medication.

items.name: monitoringProgram
items.description: The program under which the medication is reviewed.

items.name: administrationGuidelines
items.description: Guidelines for the administration of the medication.

items.name: medicineClassification
items.description: Categorization of the medication within a formulary or classification system.

items.name: packaging
items.description: Information that only applies to packages (not products).

items.name: drugCharacteristic
items.description: Specifies descriptive properties of the medicine, such as color, shape, imprints, etc.

items.name: contraindication
items.description: Potential clinical issue with or between medication(s) (for example, drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, drug-allergy interaction, etc.).

items.name: regulatory
items.description: Regulatory information about a medication.

items.name: kinetics
items.description: The time course of drug absorption, distribution, metabolism and excretion of a medication from the body.

They are business identifiers assigned to this resource by the performer or other systems and remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: status
items.description: A code specifying the current state of the order.  Generally, this will be active or completed state.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current state of the MedicationRequest.

items.name: intent
items.description: Whether the request is a proposal, plan, or an original order.

items.name: category
items.description: Indicates the type of medication request (for example, where the medication is expected to be consumed or administered (i.e. inpatient or outpatient)).

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the Medication Request should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: doNotPerform
items.description: If true indicates that the provider is asking for the medication request not to occur.

items.name: reported[x]
items.description: Indicates if this record was captured as a secondary 'reported' record rather than as an original primary source-of-truth record.  It may also indicate the source of the report.

items.name: medication[x]
items.description: Identifies the medication being requested. This is a link to a resource that represents the medication which may be the details of the medication or simply an attribute carrying a code that identifies the medication from a known list of medications.

items.name: subject
items.description: A link to a resource representing the person or set of individuals to whom the medication will be given.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this [x] was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: supportingInformation
items.description: Include additional information (for example, patient height and weight) that supports the ordering of the medication.

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: The date (and perhaps time) when the prescription was initially written or authored on.

items.name: requester
items.description: The individual, organization, or device that initiated the request and has responsibility for its activation.

items.name: performer
items.description: The specified desired performer of the medication treatment (e.g. the performer of the medication administration).

items.name: performerType
items.description: Indicates the type of performer of the administration of the medication.

items.name: recorder
items.description: The person who entered the order on behalf of another individual for example in the case of a verbal or a telephone order.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The reason or the indication for ordering or not ordering the medication.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Condition or observation that supports why the medication was ordered.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a protocol, guideline, orderset, or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this MedicationRequest.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this MedicationRequest.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A plan or request that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this medication request.

items.name: groupIdentifier
items.description: A shared identifier common to all requests that were authorized more or less simultaneously by a single author, representing the identifier of the requisition or prescription.

items.name: courseOfTherapyType
items.description: The description of the overall patte3rn of the administration of the medication to the patient.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Insurance plans, coverage extensions, pre-authorizations and/or pre-determinations that may be required for delivering the requested service.

items.name: note
items.description: Extra information about the prescription that could not be conveyed by the other attributes.

items.name: dosageInstruction
items.description: Indicates how the medication is to be used by the patient.

items.name: dispenseRequest
items.description: Indicates the specific details for the dispense or medication supply part of a medication request (also known as a Medication Prescription or Medication Order).  Note that this information is not always sent with the order.  There may be in some settings (e.g. hospitals) institutional or system support for completing the dispense details in the pharmacy department.

items.name: substitution
items.description: Indicates whether or not substitution can or should be part of the dispense. In some cases, substitution must happen, in other cases substitution must not happen. This block explains the prescriber's intent. If nothing is specified substitution may be done.

items.name: priorPrescription
items.description: A link to a resource representing an earlier order related order or prescription.

items.name: detectedIssue
items.description: Indicates an actual or potential clinical issue with or between one or more active or proposed clinical actions for a patient; e.g. Drug-drug interaction, duplicate therapy, dosage alert etc.

items.name: eventHistory
items.description: Links to Provenance records for past versions of this resource or fulfilling request or event resources that identify key state transitions or updates that are likely to be relevant to a user looking at the current version of the resource.

structure.name: MedicationStatement
structure.description:
A record of a medication that is being consumed by a patient.   A MedicationStatement may indicate that the patient may be taking the medication now or has taken the medication in the past or will be taking the medication in the future.  The source of this information can be the patient, significant other (such as a family member or spouse), or a clinician.  A common scenario where this information is captured is during the history taking process during a patient visit or stay.   The medication information may come from sources such as the patient's memory, from a prescription bottle,  or from a list of medications the patient, clinician or other party maintains. 

The primary difference between a medication statement and a medication administration is that the medication administration has complete administration information and is based on actual administration information from the person who administered the medication.  A medication statement is often, if not always, less specific.  There is no required date/time when the medication was administered, in fact we only know that a source has reported the patient is taking this medication, where details such as time, quantity, or rate or even medication product may be incomplete or missing or less precise.  As stated earlier, the medication statement information may come from the patient's memory, from a prescription bottle or from a list of medications the patient, clinician or other party maintains.  Medication administration is more formal and is not missing detailed information.

items.name: url
items.description: The business identifier that is used to reference the MessageDefinition and *is* expected to be consistent from server to server.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this message definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the message definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the message definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the message definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the message definition.

items.name: replaces
items.description: A MessageDefinition that is superseded by this definition.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this message definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this message definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the message definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the message definition changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the message definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the message definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate message definition instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the message definition is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this message definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the message definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the message definition.

items.name: base
items.description: The MessageDefinition that is the basis for the contents of this resource.

items.name: parent
items.description: Identifies a protocol or workflow that this MessageDefinition represents a step in.

items.name: event[x]
items.description: Event code or link to the EventDefinition.

items.name: category
items.description: The impact of the content of the message.

items.name: focus
items.description: Identifies the resource (or resources) that are being addressed by the event.  For example, the Encounter for an admit message or two Account records for a merge.

items.name: responseRequired
items.description: Declare at a message definition level whether a response is required or only upon error or success, or never.

items.name: allowedResponse
items.description: Indicates what types of messages may be sent as an application-level response to this message.

items.name: graph
items.description: Canonical reference to a GraphDefinition. If a URL is provided, it is the canonical reference to a [GraphDefinition](graphdefinition.html) that it controls what resources are to be added to the bundle when building the document. The GraphDefinition can also specify profiles that apply to the various resources.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier for this particular sequence instance. This is a FHIR-defined id.

items.name: type
items.description: Amino Acid Sequence/ DNA Sequence / RNA Sequence.

items.name: coordinateSystem
items.description: Whether the sequence is numbered starting at 0 (0-based numbering or coordinates, inclusive start, exclusive end) or starting at 1 (1-based numbering, inclusive start and inclusive end).

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient whose sequencing results are described by this resource.

items.name: specimen
items.description: Specimen used for sequencing.

items.name: device
items.description: The method for sequencing, for example, chip information.

items.name: performer
items.description: The organization or lab that should be responsible for this result.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of copies of the sequence of interest. (RNASeq).

items.name: referenceSeq
items.description: A sequence that is used as a reference to describe variants that are present in a sequence analyzed.

items.name: variant
items.description: The definition of variant here originates from Sequence ontology ([variant_of](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/variant_of)). This element can represent amino acid or nucleic sequence change(including insertion,deletion,SNP,etc.)  It can represent some complex mutation or segment variation with the assist of CIGAR string.

items.name: observedSeq
items.description: Sequence that was observed. It is the result marked by referenceSeq along with variant records on referenceSeq. This shall start from referenceSeq.windowStart and end by referenceSeq.windowEnd.

items.name: quality
items.description: An experimental feature attribute that defines the quality of the feature in a quantitative way, such as a phred quality score ([SO:0001686](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0001686)).

items.name: readCoverage
items.description: Coverage (read depth or depth) is the average number of reads representing a given nucleotide in the reconstructed sequence.

items.name: repository
items.description: Configurations of the external repository. The repository shall store target's observedSeq or records related with target's observedSeq.

items.name: pointer
items.description: Pointer to next atomic sequence which at most contains one variant.

items.name: structureVariant
items.description: Information about chromosome structure variation.

items.name: date

items.name: description

items.name: useContext

items.name: usage

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this order by the order sender or by the order receiver.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this NutritionOrder.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this NutritionOrder.

items.name: instantiates
items.description: The URL pointing to a protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this NutritionOrder.

items.name: status
items.description: The workflow status of the nutrition order/request.

items.name: intent
items.description: Indicates the level of authority/intentionality associated with the NutrionOrder and where the request fits into the workflow chain.

items.name: patient
items.description: The person (patient) who needs the nutrition order for an oral diet, nutritional supplement and/or enteral or formula feeding.

items.name: encounter
items.description: An encounter that provides additional information about the healthcare context in which this request is made.

items.name: dateTime
items.description: The date and time that this nutrition order was requested.

items.name: orderer
items.description: The practitioner that holds legal responsibility for ordering the diet, nutritional supplement, or formula feedings.

items.name: allergyIntolerance
items.description: A link to a record of allergies or intolerances  which should be included in the nutrition order.

items.name: foodPreferenceModifier
items.description: This modifier is used to convey order-specific modifiers about the type of food that should be given. These can be derived from patient allergies, intolerances, or preferences such as Halal, Vegan or Kosher. This modifier applies to the entire nutrition order inclusive of the oral diet, nutritional supplements and enteral formula feedings.

items.name: excludeFoodModifier
items.description: This modifier is used to convey Order-specific modifier about the type of oral food or oral fluids that should not be given. These can be derived from patient allergies, intolerances, or preferences such as No Red Meat, No Soy or No Wheat or  Gluten-Free.  While it should not be necessary to repeat allergy or intolerance information captured in the referenced AllergyIntolerance resource in the excludeFoodModifier, this element may be used to convey additional specificity related to foods that should be eliminated from the patient’s diet for any reason.  This modifier applies to the entire nutrition order inclusive of the oral diet, nutritional supplements and enteral formula feedings.

items.name: oralDiet
items.description: Diet given orally in contrast to enteral (tube) feeding.

items.name: supplement
items.description: Oral nutritional products given in order to add further nutritional value to the patient's diet.

items.name: enteralFormula
items.description: Feeding provided through the gastrointestinal tract via a tube, catheter, or stoma that delivers nutrition distal to the oral cavity.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the {{title}} by the requester, performer, subject or other participants.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this observation.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A plan, proposal or order that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this event.  For example, a MedicationRequest may require a patient to have laboratory test performed before  it is dispensed.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger event of which this particular Observation is a component or step.  For example,  an observation as part of a procedure.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the result value.

items.name: category
items.description: A code that classifies the general type of observation being made.

items.name: code
items.description: Describes what was observed. Sometimes this is called the observation "name".

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient, or group of patients, location, or device this observation is about and into whose record the observation is placed. If the actual focus of the observation is different from the subject (or a sample of, part, or region of the subject), the `focus` element or the `code` itself specifies the actual focus of the observation.

items.name: focus
items.description: The actual focus of an observation when it is not the patient of record representing something or someone associated with the patient such as a spouse, parent, fetus, or donor. For example, fetus observations in a mother's record.  The focus of an observation could also be an existing condition,  an intervention, the subject's diet,  another observation of the subject,  or a body structure such as tumor or implanted device.   An example use case would be using the Observation resource to capture whether the mother is trained to change her child's tracheostomy tube. In this example, the child is the patient of record and the mother is the focus.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The healthcare event  (e.g. a patient and healthcare provider interaction) during which this observation is made.

items.name: effective[x]
items.description: The time or time-period the observed value is asserted as being true. For biological subjects - e.g. human patients - this is usually called the "physiologically relevant time". This is usually either the time of the procedure or of specimen collection, but very often the source of the date/time is not known, only the date/time itself.

items.name: issued
items.description: The date and time this version of the observation was made available to providers, typically after the results have been reviewed and verified.

items.name: performer
items.description: Who was responsible for asserting the observed value as "true".

items.name: value[x]
items.description: The information determined as a result of making the observation, if the information has a simple value.

items.name: dataAbsentReason
items.description: Provides a reason why the expected value in the element Observation.value[x] is missing.

items.name: interpretation
items.description: A categorical assessment of an observation value.  For example, high, low, normal.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments about the observation or the results.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Indicates the site on the subject's body where the observation was made (i.e. the target site).

items.name: method
items.description: Indicates the mechanism used to perform the observation.

items.name: specimen
items.description: The specimen that was used when this observation was made.

items.name: device
items.description: The device used to generate the observation data.

items.name: referenceRange
items.description: Guidance on how to interpret the value by comparison to a normal or recommended range.  Multiple reference ranges are interpreted as an "OR".   In other words, to represent two distinct target populations, two `referenceRange` elements would be used.

items.name: hasMember
items.description: This observation is a group observation (e.g. a battery, a panel of tests, a set of vital sign measurements) that includes the target as a member of the group.

items.name: derivedFrom
items.description: The target resource that represents a measurement from which this observation value is derived. For example, a calculated anion gap or a fetal measurement based on an ultrasound image.

items.name: component
items.description: Some observations have multiple component observations.  These component observations are expressed as separate code value pairs that share the same attributes.  Examples include systolic and diastolic component observations for blood pressure measurement and multiple component observations for genetics observations.

items.name: preferredReportName

items.name: quantitativeDetails
items.description: Characteristics for quantitative results of this observation.

items.name: qualifiedInterval
items.description: Multiple  ranges of results qualified by different contexts for ordinal or continuous observations conforming to this ObservationDefinition.

items.name: validCodedValueSet

items.name: normalCodedValueSet

items.name: criticalCodedValueSet

items.name: identifier
items.description: An identifier for this patient.

items.name: active
items.description:
Whether this patient record is in active use. 
Many systems use this property to mark as non-current patients, such as those that have not been seen for a period of time based on an organization's business rules.

It is often used to filter patient lists to exclude inactive patients

Deceased patients may also be marked as inactive for the same reasons, but may be active for some time after death.

items.name: name
items.description: A name associated with the individual.

items.name: telecom
items.description: A contact detail (e.g. a telephone number or an email address) by which the individual may be contacted.

items.name: gender
items.description: Administrative Gender - the gender that the patient is considered to have for administration and record keeping purposes.

items.name: birthDate
items.description: The date of birth for the individual.

items.name: deceased[x]
items.description: Indicates if the individual is deceased or not.

items.name: address
items.description: An address for the individual.

items.name: maritalStatus
items.description: This field contains a patient's most recent marital (civil) status.

items.name: multipleBirth[x]
items.description: Indicates whether the patient is part of a multiple (boolean) or indicates the actual birth order (integer).

items.name: photo
items.description: Image of the patient.

items.name: contact
items.description: A contact party (e.g. guardian, partner, friend) for the patient.

items.name: communication
items.description: A language which may be used to communicate with the patient about his or her health.

items.name: generalPractitioner
items.description: Patient's nominated care provider.

items.name: managingOrganization
items.description: Organization that is the custodian of the patient record.

items.name: link
items.description: Link to another patient resource that concerns the same actual patient.

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this plan definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this plan definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the plan definition is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this plan definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the plan definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the plan definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. To provide a version consistent with the Decision Support Service specification, use the format Major.Minor.Revision (e.g. 1.0.0). For more information on versioning knowledge assets, refer to the Decision Support Service specification. Note that a version is required for non-experimental active artifacts.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the plan definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the plan definition.

items.name: subtitle
items.description: An explanatory or alternate title for the plan definition giving additional information about its content.

items.name: type
items.description: A high-level category for the plan definition that distinguishes the kinds of systems that would be interested in the plan definition.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this plan definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this plan definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: subject[x]
items.description: A code or group definition that describes the intended subject of the plan definition.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the plan definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the plan definition changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the plan definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the plan definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate plan definition instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the plan definition is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this plan definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: usage
items.description: A detailed description of how the plan definition is used from a clinical perspective.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the plan definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the plan definition.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the plan definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the plan definition. Topics provide a high-level categorization of the definition that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: library
items.description: A reference to a Library resource containing any formal logic used by the plan definition.

items.name: goal
items.description: Goals that describe what the activities within the plan are intended to achieve. For example, weight loss, restoring an activity of daily living, obtaining herd immunity via immunization, meeting a process improvement objective, etc.

items.name: action
items.description: An action or group of actions to be taken as part of the plan.

structure.name: Practitioner
structure.description: A person who is directly or indirectly involved in the provisioning of healthcare.

items.name: language

items.name: identifier
items.description: An identifier that applies to this person in this role.

items.name: active
items.description: Whether this practitioner's record is in active use.

items.name: name
items.description: The name(s) associated with the practitioner.

items.name: telecom
items.description: A contact detail for the practitioner, e.g. a telephone number or an email address.

items.name: address
items.description: Address(es) of the practitioner that are not role specific (typically home address). Work addresses are not typically entered in this property as they are usually role dependent.

items.name: gender
items.description: Administrative Gender - the gender that the person is considered to have for administration and record keeping purposes.

items.name: birthDate
items.description: The date of birth for the practitioner.

items.name: photo
items.description: Image of the person.

items.name: qualification
items.description: The official certifications, training, and licenses that authorize or otherwise pertain to the provision of care by the practitioner.  For example, a medical license issued by a medical board authorizing the practitioner to practice medicine within a certian locality.

items.name: communication
items.description: A language the practitioner can use in patient communication.

structure.name: PractitionerRole
structure.description: A specific set of Roles/Locations/specialties/services that a practitioner may perform at an organization for a period of time.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business Identifiers that are specific to a role/location.

items.name: active
items.description: Whether this practitioner role record is in active use.

items.name: period
items.description: The period during which the person is authorized to act as a practitioner in these role(s) for the organization.

items.name: practitioner
items.description: Practitioner that is able to provide the defined services for the organization.

items.name: organization
items.description: The organization where the Practitioner performs the roles associated.

items.name: code
items.description: Roles which this practitioner is authorized to perform for the organization.

items.name: specialty
items.description: Specific specialty of the practitioner.

items.name: location
items.description: The location(s) at which this practitioner provides care.

items.name: healthcareService
items.description: The list of healthcare services that this worker provides for this role's Organization/Location(s).

items.name: telecom
items.description: Contact details that are specific to the role/location/service.

items.name: availableTime
items.description: A collection of times the practitioner is available or performing this role at the location and/or healthcareservice.

items.name: notAvailable
items.description: The practitioner is not available or performing this role during this period of time due to the provided reason.

items.name: availabilityExceptions
items.description: A description of site availability exceptions, e.g. public holiday availability. Succinctly describing all possible exceptions to normal site availability as details in the available Times and not available Times.

items.name: endpoint
items.description: Technical endpoints providing access to services operated for the practitioner with this role.

structure.name: Procedure
structure.description: An action that is or was performed on or for a patient. This can be a physical intervention like an operation, or less invasive like long term services, counseling, or hypnotherapy.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.
