items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this procedure by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and is propagated from server to server.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, order set or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Procedure.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, order set or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Procedure.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A reference to a resource that contains details of the request for this procedure.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger event of which this particular procedure is a component or step.

items.name: status
items.description: A code specifying the state of the procedure. Generally, this will be the in-progress or completed state.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Captures the reason for the current state of the procedure.

items.name: category
items.description: A code that classifies the procedure for searching, sorting and display purposes (e.g. "Surgical Procedure").

items.name: code
items.description: The specific procedure that is performed. Use text if the exact nature of the procedure cannot be coded (e.g. "Laparoscopic Appendectomy").

items.name: subject
items.description: The person, animal or group on which the procedure was performed.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this Procedure was created or performed or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: performed[x]
items.description: Estimated or actual date, date-time, period, or age when the procedure was performed.  Allows a period to support complex procedures that span more than one date, and also allows for the length of the procedure to be captured.

items.name: recorder
items.description: Individual who recorded the record and takes responsibility for its content.

items.name: asserter
items.description: Individual who is making the procedure statement.

items.name: performer
items.description: Limited to "real" people rather than equipment.

items.name: location
items.description: The location where the procedure actually happened.  E.g. a newborn at home, a tracheostomy at a restaurant.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The coded reason why the procedure was performed. This may be a coded entity of some type, or may simply be present as text.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: The justification of why the procedure was performed.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Detailed and structured anatomical location information. Multiple locations are allowed - e.g. multiple punch biopsies of a lesion.

items.name: outcome
items.description: The outcome of the procedure - did it resolve the reasons for the procedure being performed?

items.name: report
items.description: This could be a histology result, pathology report, surgical report, etc.

items.name: complication
items.description: Any complications that occurred during the procedure, or in the immediate post-performance period. These are generally tracked separately from the notes, which will typically describe the procedure itself rather than any 'post procedure' issues.

items.name: complicationDetail
items.description: Any complications that occurred during the procedure, or in the immediate post-performance period.

items.name: followUp
items.description: If the procedure required specific follow up - e.g. removal of sutures. The follow up may be represented as a simple note or could potentially be more complex, in which case the CarePlan resource can be used.

items.name: note
items.description: Any other notes and comments about the procedure.

items.name: focalDevice
items.description: A device that is implanted, removed or otherwise manipulated (calibration, battery replacement, fitting a prosthesis, attaching a wound-vac, etc.) as a focal portion of the Procedure.

items.name: usedReference
items.description: Identifies medications, devices and any other substance used as part of the procedure.

items.name: usedCode
items.description: Identifies coded items that were used as part of the procedure.

structure.name: Provenance
structure.description: Provenance of a resource is a record that describes entities and processes involved in producing and delivering or otherwise influencing that resource. Provenance provides a critical foundation for assessing authenticity, enabling trust, and allowing reproducibility. Provenance assertions are a form of contextual metadata and can themselves become important records with their own provenance. Provenance statement indicates clinical significance in terms of confidence in authenticity, reliability, and trustworthiness, integrity, and stage in lifecycle (e.g. Document Completion - has the artifact been legally authenticated), all of which may impact security, privacy, and trust policies.

items.name: target
items.description: The Reference(s) that were generated or updated by  the activity described in this resource. A provenance can point to more than one target if multiple resources were created/updated by the same activity.

items.name: occurred[x]
items.description: The period during which the activity occurred.

items.name: recorded
items.description: The instant of time at which the activity was recorded.

items.name: policy
items.description: Policy or plan the activity was defined by. Typically, a single activity may have multiple applicable policy documents, such as patient consent, guarantor funding, etc.

items.name: location
items.description: Where the activity occurred, if relevant.

items.name: reason
items.description: The reason that the activity was taking place.

items.name: activity
items.description: An activity is something that occurs over a period of time and acts upon or with entities; it may include consuming, processing, transforming, modifying, relocating, using, or generating entities.

items.name: agent
items.description: An actor taking a role in an activity  for which it can be assigned some degree of responsibility for the activity taking place.

items.name: entity
items.description: An entity used in this activity.

items.name: signature
items.description: A digital signature on the target Reference(s). The signer should match a Provenance.agent. The purpose of the signature is indicated.

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this questionnaire when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this questionnaire is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the questionnaire is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this questionnaire when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the questionnaire when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the questionnaire author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the questionnaire. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the questionnaire.

items.name: derivedFrom
items.description: The URL of a Questionnaire that this Questionnaire is based on.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this questionnaire. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this questionnaire is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: subjectType
items.description: The types of subjects that can be the subject of responses created for the questionnaire.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the questionnaire was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the questionnaire changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the questionnaire.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the questionnaire from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: lastReviewDate

items.name: effectivePeriod

items.name: code

items.name: item
items.description: A particular question, question grouping or display text that is part of the questionnaire.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A business identifier assigned to a particular completed (or partially completed) questionnaire.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: The order, proposal or plan that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this QuestionnaireResponse.  For example, a ServiceRequest seeking an intake assessment or a decision support recommendation to assess for post-partum depression.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A procedure or observation that this questionnaire was performed as part of the execution of.  For example, the surgery a checklist was executed as part of.

items.name: questionnaire
items.description: The Questionnaire that defines and organizes the questions for which answers are being provided.

items.name: status
items.description: The position of the questionnaire response within its overall lifecycle.

items.name: subject
items.description: The subject of the questionnaire response.  This could be a patient, organization, practitioner, device, etc.  This is who/what the answers apply to, but is not necessarily the source of information.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounter during which this questionnaire response was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: authored
items.description: The date and/or time that this set of answers were last changed.

items.name: author
items.description: Person who received the answers to the questions in the QuestionnaireResponse and recorded them in the system.

items.name: source
items.description: The person who answered the questions about the subject.

items.name: item
items.description: A group or question item from the original questionnaire for which answers are provided.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifier for a person within a particular scope.

items.name: active
items.description: Whether this related person record is in active use.

items.name: patient
items.description: The patient this person is related to.

items.name: relationship
items.description: The nature of the relationship between a patient and the related person.

items.name: name
items.description: A name associated with the person.

items.name: telecom
items.description: A contact detail for the person, e.g. a telephone number or an email address.

items.name: gender
items.description: Administrative Gender - the gender that the person is considered to have for administration and record keeping purposes.

items.name: birthDate
items.description: The date on which the related person was born.

items.name: address
items.description: Address where the related person can be contacted or visited.

items.name: photo
items.description: Image of the person.

items.name: period
items.description: The period of time during which this relationship is or was active. If there are no dates defined, then the interval is unknown.

items.name: communication
items.description: A language which may be used to communicate with about the patient's health.

structure.name: RequestGroup
structure.description: A group of related requests that can be used to capture intended activities that have inter-dependencies such as "give this medication after that one".

items.name: language

items.name: identifier
items.description: Allows a service to provide a unique, business identifier for the request.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: A canonical URL referencing a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this request.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: A URL referencing an externally defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this request.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A plan, proposal or order that is fulfilled in whole or in part by this request.

items.name: replaces
items.description: Completed or terminated request(s) whose function is taken by this new request.

items.name: groupIdentifier
items.description: A shared identifier common to all requests that were authorized more or less simultaneously by a single author, representing the identifier of the requisition, prescription or similar form.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the request. For request groups, the status reflects the status of all the requests in the group.

items.name: intent
items.description: Indicates the level of authority/intentionality associated with the request and where the request fits into the workflow chain.

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the request should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: code
items.description: A code that identifies what the overall request group is.

items.name: subject
items.description: The subject for which the request group was created.

items.name: encounter
items.description: Describes the context of the request group, if any.

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: Indicates when the request group was created.

items.name: author
items.description: Provides a reference to the author of the request group.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Describes the reason for the request group in coded or textual form.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource whose existence justifies this request group.

items.name: note
items.description: Provides a mechanism to communicate additional information about the response.

items.name: action
items.description: The actions, if any, produced by the evaluation of the artifact.

items.name: url
items.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this research definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this research definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the research definition is stored on different servers.

items.name: identifier
items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this research definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the research definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the research definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. To provide a version consistent with the Decision Support Service specification, use the format Major.Minor.Revision (e.g. 1.0.0). For more information on versioning knowledge assets, refer to the Decision Support Service specification. Note that a version is required for non-experimental active artifacts.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the research definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the research definition.

items.name: shortTitle
items.description: The short title provides an alternate title for use in informal descriptive contexts where the full, formal title is not necessary.

items.name: subtitle
items.description: An explanatory or alternate title for the ResearchDefinition giving additional information about its content.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this research definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this research definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: subject[x]
items.description: The intended subjects for the ResearchDefinition. If this element is not provided, a Patient subject is assumed, but the subject of the ResearchDefinition can be anything.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the research definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the research definition changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the research definition.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the research definition from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: comment
items.description: A human-readable string to clarify or explain concepts about the resource.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate research definition instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the research definition is intended to be used.

items.name: purpose
items.description: Explanation of why this research definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

items.name: usage
items.description: A detailed description, from a clinical perspective, of how the ResearchDefinition is used.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the research definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the research definition.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the research definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the ResearchDefinition. Topics provide a high-level categorization grouping types of ResearchDefinitions that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: library
items.description: A reference to a Library resource containing the formal logic used by the ResearchDefinition.

items.name: population
items.description: A reference to a ResearchElementDefinition resource that defines the population for the research.

items.name: exposure
items.description: A reference to a ResearchElementDefinition resource that defines the exposure for the research.

items.name: exposureAlternative
items.description: A reference to a ResearchElementDefinition resource that defines the exposureAlternative for the research.

items.name: outcome
items.description: A reference to a ResearchElementDefinition resomece that defines the outcome for the research.

structure.name: ResearchStudy
structure.description: A process where a researcher or organization plans and then executes a series of steps intended to increase the field of healthcare-related knowledge.  This includes studies of safety, efficacy, comparative effectiveness and other information about medications, devices, therapies and other interventional and investigative techniques.  A ResearchStudy involves the gathering of information about human or animal subjects.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this research study by the sponsor or other systems.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive user-friendly label for the study.

items.name: protocol
items.description: The set of steps expected to be performed as part of the execution of the study.

items.name: partOf
items.description: A larger research study of which this particular study is a component or step.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the study.

items.name: primaryPurposeType
items.description: The type of study based upon the intent of the study's activities. A classification of the intent of the study.

items.name: phase
items.description: The stage in the progression of a therapy from initial experimental use in humans in clinical trials to post-market evaluation.

items.name: category
items.description: Codes categorizing the type of study such as investigational vs. observational, type of blinding, type of randomization, safety vs. efficacy, etc.

items.name: focus
items.description: The medication(s), food(s), therapy(ies), device(s) or other concerns or interventions that the study is seeking to gain more information about.

items.name: condition
items.description: The condition that is the focus of the study.  For example, In a study to examine risk factors for Lupus, might have as an inclusion criterion "healthy volunteer", but the target condition code would be a Lupus SNOMED code.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in learning more about or engaging with the study.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Citations, references and other related documents.

items.name: keyword
items.description: Key terms to aid in searching for or filtering the study.

items.name: location
items.description: Indicates a country, state or other region where the study is taking place.

items.name: description
items.description: A full description of how the study is being conducted.

items.name: enrollment
items.description: Reference to a Group that defines the criteria for and quantity of subjects participating in the study.  E.g. " 200 female Europeans between the ages of 20 and 45 with early onset diabetes".

items.name: period
items.description: Identifies the start date and the expected (or actual, depending on status) end date for the study.

items.name: sponsor
items.description: An organization that initiates the investigation and is legally responsible for the study.

items.name: principalInvestigator
items.description: A researcher in a study who oversees multiple aspects of the study, such as concept development, protocol writing, protocol submission for IRB approval, participant recruitment, informed consent, data collection, analysis, interpretation and presentation.

items.name: site
items.description: A facility in which study activities are conducted.

items.name: reasonStopped
items.description: A description and/or code explaining the premature termination of the study.

items.name: note
items.description: Comments made about the study by the performer, subject or other participants.

items.name: arm
items.description: Describes an expected sequence of events for one of the participants of a study.  E.g. Exposure to drug A, wash-out, exposure to drug B, wash-out, follow-up.

items.name: objective
items.description: A goal that the study is aiming to achieve in terms of a scientific question to be answered by the analysis of data collected during the study.

structure.name: ResearchSubject
structure.description: A physical entity which is the primary unit of operational and/or administrative interest in a study.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this research subject for a study.

items.name: status
items.description: The current state of the subject.

items.name: period
items.description: The dates the subject began and ended their participation in the study.

items.name: study
items.description: Reference to the study the subject is participating in.

items.name: individual
items.description: The record of the person or animal who is involved in the study.

items.name: assignedArm
items.description: The name of the arm in the study the subject is expected to follow as part of this study.

items.name: actualArm
items.description: The name of the arm in the study the subject actually followed as part of this study.

items.name: consent

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifier assigned to the risk assessment.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: A reference to the request that is fulfilled by this risk assessment.

items.name: parent
items.description: A reference to a resource that this risk assessment is part of, such as a Procedure.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the RiskAssessment, using the same statuses as an Observation.

items.name: method
items.description: The algorithm, process or mechanism used to evaluate the risk.

items.name: code
items.description: The type of the risk assessment performed.

items.name: subject
items.description: The patient or group the risk assessment applies to.

items.name: encounter
items.description: The encounter where the assessment was performed.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: The date (and possibly time) the risk assessment was performed.

items.name: condition
items.description: For assessments or prognosis specific to a particular condition, indicates the condition being assessed.

items.name: performer
items.description: The provider or software application that performed the assessment.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The reason the risk assessment was performed.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Resources supporting the reason the risk assessment was performed.

items.name: basis
items.description: Indicates the source data considered as part of the assessment (for example, FamilyHistory, Observations, Procedures, Conditions, etc.).

items.name: prediction
items.description: Describes the expected outcome for the subject.

items.name: mitigation
items.description: A description of the steps that might be taken to reduce the identified risk(s).

items.name: note
items.description: Additional comments about the risk assessment.

items.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this risk evidence synthesis when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

items.description: A natural language name identifying the risk evidence synthesis. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the risk evidence synthesis.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this risk evidence synthesis. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the risk evidence synthesis was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the risk evidence synthesis changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the risk evidence synthesis.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description

items.name: note

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate risk evidence synthesis instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the risk evidence synthesis is intended to be used.

items.name: copyright
items.description: A copyright statement relating to the risk evidence synthesis and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the risk evidence synthesis.

items.name: approvalDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

items.name: lastReviewDate
items.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

items.name: effectivePeriod
items.description: The period during which the risk evidence synthesis content was or is planned to be in active use.

items.name: topic
items.description: Descriptive topics related to the content of the RiskEvidenceSynthesis. Topics provide a high-level categorization grouping types of EffectEvidenceSynthesiss that can be useful for filtering and searching.

items.name: author
items.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

items.name: editor
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

items.name: reviewer
items.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

items.name: endorser
items.description: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

items.name: relatedArtifact
items.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

items.name: synthesisType
items.description: Type of synthesis eg meta-analysis.

items.name: studyType
items.description: Type of study eg randomized trial.

items.name: population
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resource that defines the population for the research.

items.name: exposure
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resource that defines the exposure for the research.

items.name: outcome
items.description: A reference to a EvidenceVariable resomece that defines the outcome for the research.

items.name: sampleSize
items.description: A description of the size of the sample involved in the synthesis.

items.name: riskEstimate
items.description: The estimated risk of the outcome.

items.name: certainty
items.description: A description of the certainty of the risk estimate.

items.name: planningHorizon
items.description: The period of time that the slots that reference this Schedule resource cover (even if none exist). items.name: comment

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifiers assigned to this order instance by the orderer and/or the receiver and/or order fulfiller.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this ServiceRequest.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this ServiceRequest.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: Plan/proposal/order fulfilled by this request.

items.name: replaces
items.description: The request takes the place of the referenced completed or terminated request(s).

items.name: requisition
items.description: A shared identifier common to all service requests that were authorized more or less simultaneously by a single author, representing the composite or group identifier.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the order.

items.name: intent
items.description: Whether the request is a proposal, plan, an original order or a reflex order.

items.name: category
items.description: A code that classifies the service for searching, sorting and display purposes (e.g. "Surgical Procedure").

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the ServiceRequest should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: doNotPerform
items.description: Set this to true if the record is saying that the service/procedure should NOT be performed.

items.name: code
items.description: A code that identifies a particular service (i.e., procedure, diagnostic investigation, or panel of investigations) that have been requested.

items.name: orderDetail
items.description: Additional details and instructions about the how the services are to be delivered.   For example, and order for a urinary catheter may have an order detail for an external or indwelling catheter, or an order for a bandage may require additional instructions specifying how the bandage should be applied.

items.name: quantity[x]
items.description: An amount of service being requested which can be a quantity ( for example $1,500 home modification), a ratio ( for example, 20 half day visits per month), or a range (2.0 to 1.8 Gy per fraction).

items.name: subject
items.description: On whom or what the service is to be performed. This is usually a human patient, but can also be requested on animals, groups of humans or animals, devices such as dialysis machines, or even locations (typically for environmental scans).

items.name: encounter
items.description: An encounter that provides additional information about the healthcare context in which this request is made.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: The date/time at which the requested service should occur.

items.name: asNeeded[x]
items.description: If a CodeableConcept is present, it indicates the pre-condition for performing the service.  For example "pain", "on flare-up", etc.

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: When the request transitioned to being actionable.

items.name: requester
items.description: The individual who initiated the request and has responsibility for its activation.

items.name: performerType
items.description: Desired type of performer for doing the requested service.

items.name: performer
items.description: The desired performer for doing the requested service.  For example, the surgeon, dermatopathologist, endoscopist, etc.

items.name: locationCode
items.description: The preferred location(s) where the procedure should actually happen in coded or free text form. E.g. at home or nursing day care center.

items.name: locationReference
items.description: A reference to the the preferred location(s) where the procedure should actually happen. E.g. at home or nursing day care center.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested in coded or textual form.   This is often for billing purposes.  May relate to the resources referred to in `supportingInfo`.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource that provides a justification for why this service is being requested.   May relate to the resources referred to in `supportingInfo`.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Insurance plans, coverage extensions, pre-authorizations and/or pre-determinations that may be needed for delivering the requested service.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional clinical information about the patient or specimen that may influence the services or their interpretations.     This information includes diagnosis, clinical findings and other observations.  In laboratory ordering these are typically referred to as "ask at order entry questions (AOEs)".  This includes observations explicitly requested by the producer (filler) to provide context or supporting information needed to complete the order. For example,  reporting the amount of inspired oxygen for blood gas measurements.

items.name: specimen
items.description: One or more specimens that the laboratory procedure will use.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Anatomic location where the procedure should be performed. This is the target site.

items.name: note
items.description: Any other notes and comments made about the service request. For example, internal billing notes.

items.name: patientInstruction
items.description: Instructions in terms that are understood by the patient or consumer.

items.name: relevantHistory
items.description: Key events in the history of the request.

items.description: The style of appointment or patient that may be booked in the slot (not service type).

items.name: schedule
items.description: The schedule resource that this slot defines an interval of status information.

items.name: status
items.description: busy | free | busy-unavailable | busy-tentative | entered-in-error.

items.name: start
items.description: Date/Time that the slot is to begin.

items.name: end
items.description: Date/Time that the slot is to conclude.

items.name: overbooked
items.description: This slot has already been overbooked, appointments are unlikely to be accepted for this time.

items.name: comment

items.name: identifier
items.description: Id for specimen.

items.name: accessionIdentifier
items.description: The identifier assigned by the lab when accessioning specimen(s). This is not necessarily the same as the specimen identifier, depending on local lab procedures.

items.name: status
items.description: The availability of the specimen.

items.name: type
items.description: The kind of material that forms the specimen.

items.name: subject
items.description: Where the specimen came from. This may be from patient(s), from a location (e.g., the source of an environmental sample), or a sampling of a substance or a device.

items.name: receivedTime
items.description: Time when specimen was received for processing or testing.

items.name: parent
items.description: Reference to the parent (source) specimen which is used when the specimen was either derived from or a component of another specimen.

items.name: request
items.description: Details concerning a service request that required a specimen to be collected.

items.name: collection
items.description: Details concerning the specimen collection.

items.name: processing
items.description: Details concerning processing and processing steps for the specimen.

items.name: container
items.description: The container holding the specimen.  The recursive nature of containers; i.e. blood in tube in tray in rack is not addressed here.

items.name: condition
items.description: A mode or state of being that describes the nature of the specimen.

items.name: note
items.description: To communicate any details or issues about the specimen or during the specimen collection. (for example: broken vial, sent with patient, frozen).

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this structure definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this structure definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

Once a subscription is registered with the server, the server checks every resource that is created or updated, and if the resource matches the given criteria, it sends a message on the defined "channel" so that another system can take an appropriate action.

items.name: sequenceType
items.description: The type of the sequence shall be specified based on a controlled vocabulary.

items.name: numberOfSubunits
items.description: The number of linear sequences of nucleotides linked through phosphodiester bonds shall be described. Subunits would be strands of nucleic acids that are tightly associated typically through Watson-Crick base pairing. NOTE: If not specified in the reference source, the assumption is that there is 1 subunit.

items.name: areaOfHybridisation
items.description: The area of hybridisation shall be described if applicable for double stranded RNA or DNA. The number associated with the subunit followed by the number associated to the residue shall be specified in increasing order. The underscore “” shall be used as separator as follows: “Subunitnumber Residue”.

items.name: oligoNucleotideType
items.description: (TBC).

items.name: subunit
items.description: Subunits are listed in order of decreasing length; sequences of the same length will be ordered by molecular weight; subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated multiple times.

structure.name: SubstanceProtein
structure.description: A SubstanceProtein is defined as a single unit of a linear amino acid sequence, or a combination of subunits that are either covalently linked or have a defined invariant stoichiometric relationship. This includes all synthetic, recombinant and purified SubstanceProteins of defined sequence, whether the use is therapeutic or prophylactic. This set of elements will be used to describe albumins, coagulation factors, cytokines, growth factors, peptide/SubstanceProtein hormones, enzymes, toxins, toxoids, recombinant vaccines, and immunomodulators.

items.name: sequenceType
items.description: The SubstanceProtein descriptive elements will only be used when a complete or partial amino acid sequence is available or derivable from a nucleic acid sequence.

items.name: numberOfSubunits
items.description: Number of linear sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds. The number of subunits constituting the SubstanceProtein shall be described. It is possible that the number of subunits can be variable.

items.name: disulfideLinkage
items.description: The disulphide bond between two cysteine residues either on the same subunit or on two different subunits shall be described. The position of the disulfide bonds in the SubstanceProtein shall be listed in increasing order of subunit number and position within subunit followed by the abbreviation of the amino acids involved. The disulfide linkage positions shall actually contain the amino acid Cysteine at the respective positions.

items.name: subunit
items.description: This subclause refers to the description of each subunit constituting the SubstanceProtein. A subunit is a linear sequence of amino acids linked through peptide bonds. The Subunit information shall be provided when the finished SubstanceProtein is a complex of multiple sequences; subunits are not used to delineate domains within a single sequence. Subunits are listed in order of decreasing length; sequences of the same length will be ordered by decreasing molecular weight; subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated multiple times.

structure.name: SubstanceSourceMaterial
structure.description: Source material shall capture information on the taxonomic and anatomical origins as well as the fraction of a material that can result in or can be modified to form a substance. This set of data elements shall be used to define polymer substances isolated from biological matrices. Taxonomic and anatomical origins shall be described using a controlled vocabulary as required. This information is captured for naturally derived polymers ( . starch) and structurally diverse substances. For Organisms belonging to the Kingdom Plantae the Substance level defines the fresh material of a single species or infraspecies, the Herbal Drug and the Herbal preparation. For Herbal preparations, the fraction information will be captured at the Substance information level and additional information for herbal extracts will be captured at the Specified Substance Group 1 information level. See for further explanation the Substance Class: Structurally Diverse and the herbal annex.

items.name: sourceMaterialClass
items.description: General high level classification of the source material specific to the origin of the material.

items.name: sourceMaterialType
items.description: The type of the source material shall be specified based on a controlled vocabulary. For vaccines, this subclause refers to the class of infectious agent.

items.name: sourceMaterialState
items.description: The state of the source material when extracted.

items.name: organismId
items.description: The unique identifier associated with the source material parent organism shall be specified.

items.name: organismName
items.description: The organism accepted Scientific name shall be provided based on the organism taxonomy.

items.name: parentSubstanceId
items.description: The parent of the herbal drug Ginkgo biloba, Leaf is the substance ID of the substance (fresh) of Ginkgo biloba L. or Ginkgo biloba L. (Whole plant).

items.name: parentSubstanceName
items.description: The parent substance of the Herbal Drug, or Herbal preparation.

items.name: countryOfOrigin
items.description: The country where the plant material is harvested or the countries where the plasma is sourced from as laid down in accordance with the Plasma Master File. For “Plasma-derived substances” the attribute country of origin provides information about the countries used for the manufacturing of the Cryopoor plama or Crioprecipitate.

items.name: geographicalLocation
items.description: The place/region where the plant is harvested or the places/regions where the animal source material has its habitat.

items.name: developmentStage
items.description: Stage of life for animals, plants, insects and microorganisms. This information shall be provided only when the substance is significantly different in these stages (e.g. foetal bovine serum).

items.name: fractionDescription
items.description: Many complex materials are fractions of parts of plants, animals, or minerals. Fraction elements are often necessary to define both Substances and Specified Group 1 Substances. For substances derived from Plants, fraction information will be captured at the Substance information level ( . Oils, Juices and Exudates). Additional information for Extracts, such as extraction solvent composition, will be captured at the Specified Substance Group 1 information level. For plasma-derived products fraction information will be captured at the Substance and the Specified Substance Group 1 levels.

items.name: organism
items.description: This subclause describes the organism which the substance is derived from. For vaccines, the parent organism shall be specified based on these subclause elements. As an example, full taxonomy will be described for the Substance Name: ., Leaf.

items.name: partDescription
items.description: To do.

items.name: destination

items.name: receiver

items.name: identifier
items.description: Business identifiers assigned to this SupplyRequest by the author and/or other systems. These identifiers remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server.

items.name: status
items.description: Status of the supply request.

items.name: category
items.description: Category of supply, e.g.  central, non-stock, etc. This is used to support work flows associated with the supply process.

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly this SupplyRequest should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: item[x]
items.description: The item that is requested to be supplied. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the item or a code that identifies the item from a known list.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The amount that is being ordered of the indicated item.

items.name: parameter
items.description: Specific parameters for the ordered item.  For example, the size of the indicated item.

items.name: occurrence[x]
items.description: When the request should be fulfilled.

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: When the request was made.

items.name: requester
items.description: The device, practitioner, etc. who initiated the request.

items.name: supplier
items.description: Who is intended to fulfill the request.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: The reason why the supply item was requested.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: The reason why the supply item was requested.

items.name: deliverFrom
items.description: Where the supply is expected to come from.

items.name: deliverTo
items.description: Where the supply is destined to go.

items.name: identifier
items.description: The business identifier for this task.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a *FHIR*-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Task.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an *externally* maintained  protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Task.

items.name: basedOn
items.description: BasedOn refers to a higher-level authorization that triggered the creation of the task.  It references a "request" resource such as a ServiceRequest, MedicationRequest, ServiceRequest, CarePlan, etc. which is distinct from the "request" resource the task is seeking to fulfill.  This latter resource is referenced by FocusOn.  For example, based on a ServiceRequest (= BasedOn), a task is created to fulfill a procedureRequest ( = FocusOn ) to collect a specimen from a patient.

items.name: groupIdentifier
items.description: An identifier that links together multiple tasks and other requests that were created in the same context.

items.name: partOf
items.description: Task that this particular task is part of.

items.name: status
items.description: The current status of the task.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: An explanation as to why this task is held, failed, was refused, etc.

items.name: businessStatus
items.description: Contains business-specific nuances of the business state.

items.name: intent
items.description: Indicates the "level" of actionability associated with the Task, i.e. i+R[9]Cs this a proposed task, a planned task, an actionable task, etc.

items.name: priority
items.description: Indicates how quickly the Task should be addressed with respect to other requests.

items.name: code
items.description: A name or code (or both) briefly describing what the task involves.

items.name: description
items.description: A free-text description of what is to be performed.

items.name: focus
items.description: The request being actioned or the resource being manipulated by this task.

items.name: for
items.description: The entity who benefits from the performance of the service specified in the task (e.g., the patient).

items.name: encounter
items.description: The healthcare event  (e.g. a patient and healthcare provider interaction) during which this task was created.

items.name: executionPeriod
items.description: Identifies the time action was first taken against the task (start) and/or the time final action was taken against the task prior to marking it as completed (end).

items.name: authoredOn
items.description: The date and time this task was created.

items.name: lastModified
items.description: The date and time of last modification to this task.

items.name: requester
items.description: The creator of the task.

items.name: performerType
items.description: The kind of participant that should perform the task.

items.name: owner
items.description: Individual organization or Device currently responsible for task execution.

items.name: location
items.description: Principal physical location where the this task is performed.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: A description or code indicating why this task needs to be performed.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: A resource reference indicating why this task needs to be performed.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Insurance plans, coverage extensions, pre-authorizations and/or pre-determinations that may be relevant to the Task.

items.name: note
items.description: Free-text information captured about the task as it progresses.

items.name: relevantHistory
items.description: Links to Provenance records for past versions of this Task that identify key state transitions or updates that are likely to be relevant to a user looking at the current version of the task.

items.name: restriction
items.description: If the Task.focus is a request resource and the task is seeking fulfillment (i.e. is asking for the request to be actioned), this element identifies any limitations on what parts of the referenced request should be actioned.

items.name: input
items.description: Additional information that may be needed in the execution of the task.

items.name: output
items.description: Outputs produced by the Task.

items.description: A value set can also be "expanded", where the value set is turned into a simple collection of enumerated codes. This element holds the expansion, if it has been performed.

This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this metadata resource is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the metadata resource is stored on different servers.

items.name: version
items.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the metadata resource when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the metadata resource author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

items.name: name
items.description: A natural language name identifying the metadata resource. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

items.name: title
items.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the metadata resource.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of this metadata resource. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

items.name: experimental
items.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this metadata resource is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

items.name: date
items.description: The date  (and optionally time) when the metadata resource was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the metadata resource changes.

items.name: publisher
items.description: The name of the organization or individual that published the metadata resource.

items.name: contact
items.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

items.name: description
items.description: A free text natural language description of the metadata resource from a consumer's perspective.

items.name: useContext
items.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate metadata resource instances.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: A legal or geographic region in which the metadata resource is intended to be used.

The party(s) that contribute to payment (or part of) of the charges applied to this account (including self-pay).

items.name: substance
items.description: Identification of the specific substance (or pharmaceutical product) considered to be responsible for the Adverse Reaction event. Note: the substance for a specific reaction may be different from the substance identified as the cause of the risk, but it must be consistent with it. For instance, it may be a more specific substance (e.g. a brand medication) or a composite product that includes the identified substance. It must be clinically safe to only process the 'code' and ignore the 'reaction.substance'.  If a receiving system is unable to confirm that AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance falls within the semantic scope of AllergyIntolerance.code, then the receiving system should ignore AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance.

items.name: manifestation
items.description: Clinical symptoms and/or signs that are observed or associated with the adverse reaction event.

items.name: description
items.description: Text description about the reaction as a whole, including details of the manifestation if required.

items.name: onset
items.description: Record of the date and/or time of the onset of the Reaction.

items.name: severity
items.description: Clinical assessment of the severity of the reaction event as a whole, potentially considering multiple different manifestations.

items.name: exposureRoute
items.description: Identification of the route by which the subject was exposed to the substance.

items.name: note
items.description: Additional text about the adverse reaction event not captured in other fields.

items.description: Identifies the outcome at the point when the status of the activity is assessed.  For example, the outcome of an education activity could be patient understands (or not).

items.description: Details of the outcome or action resulting from the activity.  The reference to an "event" resource, such as Procedure or Encounter or Observation, is the result/outcome of the activity itself.  The activity can be conveyed using CarePlan.activity.detail OR using the CarePlan.activity.reference (a reference to a “request” resource).

items.description: The details of the proposed activity represented in a specific resource.

items.name: detail
items.description: A simple summary of a planned activity suitable for a general care plan system (e.g. form driven) that doesn't know about specific resources such as procedure etc.

items.name: priceComponent
items.description: The price for a ChargeItem may be calculated as a base price with surcharges/deductions that apply in certain conditions. A ChargeItemDefinition resource that defines the prices, factors and conditions that apply to a billing code is currently under development. The priceComponent element can be used to offer transparency to the recipient of the Invoice of how the prices have been calculated.

items.name: deviceIdentifier
items.description: The device identifier (DI) is a mandatory, fixed portion of a UDI that identifies the labeler and the specific version or model of a device.

items.name: issuer
items.description:
Organization that is charged with issuing UDIs for devices.  For example, the US FDA issuers include :
1) GS1: 
http://hl7.org/fhir/NamingSystem/gs1-di, 
2) HIBCC:
http://hl7.org/fhir/NamingSystem/hibcc-dI, 
3) ICCBBA for blood containers:
http://hl7.org/fhir/NamingSystem/iccbba-blood-di, 
4) ICCBA for other devices:
http://hl7.org/fhir/NamingSystem/iccbba-other-di.

items.name: jurisdiction
items.description: The identity of the authoritative source for UDI generation within a  jurisdiction.  All UDIs are globally unique within a single namespace with the appropriate repository uri as the system.  For example,  UDIs of devices managed in the U.S. by the FDA, the value is  http://hl7.org/fhir/NamingSystem/fda-udi.

items.name: carrierAIDC
items.description: The full UDI carrier of the Automatic Identification and Data Capture (AIDC) technology representation of the barcode string as printed on the packaging of the device - e.g., a barcode or RFID.   Because of limitations on character sets in XML and the need to round-trip JSON data through XML, AIDC Formats *SHALL* be base64 encoded.

items.name: carrierHRF
items.description: The full UDI carrier as the human readable form (HRF) representation of the barcode string as printed on the packaging of the device.

items.name: entryType

structure.name: Encounter_ClassHistory
structure.description: The class history permits the tracking of the encounters transitions without needing to go  through the resource history.  This would be used for a case where an admission starts of as an emergency encounter, then transitions into an inpatient scenario. Doing this and not restarting a new encounter ensures that any lab/diagnostic results can more easily follow the patient and not require re-processing and not get lost or cancelled during a kind of discharge from emergency to inpatient.

items.description: The type of revenue or cost center providing the product and/or service.

items.name: category
items.description: Code to identify the general type of benefits under which products and services are provided.

items.name: productOrService
items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.name: modifier
items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: programCode
items.description: Identifies the program under which this may be recovered.

items.description: The date or dates when the service or product was supplied, performed or completed.

items.name: location[x]
items.description: Where the product or service was provided.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: udi
items.description: Unique Device Identifiers associated with this line item.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Physical service site on the patient (limb, tooth, etc.).

items.name: subSite
items.description: A region or surface of the bodySite, e.g. limb region or tooth surface(s).

items.name: encounter

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net

items.description: The target value of the focus to be achieved to signify the fulfillment of the goal, e.g. 150 pounds, 7.0%. Either the high or low or both values of the range can be specified. When a low value is missing, it indicates that the goal is achieved at any focus value at or below the high value. Similarly, if the high value is missing, it indicates that the goal is achieved at any focus value at or above the low value.

items.description: The modality of this series sequence.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: The anatomic structures examined. See DICOM Part 16 Annex L (http://dicom.nema.org/medical/dicom/current/output/chtml/part16/chapter_L.html) for DICOM to SNOMED-CT mappings. The bodySite may indicate the laterality of body part imaged; if so, it shall be consistent with any content of ImagingStudy.series.laterality.

items.name: laterality
items.description: The laterality of the (possibly paired) anatomic structures examined. E.g., the left knee, both lungs, or unpaired abdomen. If present, shall be consistent with any laterality information indicated in ImagingStudy.series.bodySite.

items.name: specimen

items.name: started

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates who or what performed the series and how they were involved.

items.name: instance
items.description: A single SOP instance within the series, e.g. an image, or presentation state.

items.name: code
items.description: Indicates a meaning for the supplemental data. This can be as simple as a unique identifier, or it can establish meaning in a broader context by drawing from a terminology, allowing supplemental data to be correlated across measures.

items.name: usage
items.description: An indicator of the intended usage for the supplemental data element. Supplemental data indicates the data is additional information requested to augment the measure information. Risk adjustment factor indicates the data is additional information used to calculate risk adjustment factors when applying a risk model to the measure calculation.

items.name: description
items.description: The human readable description of this supplemental data.

items.name: criteria
items.description: The criteria for the supplemental data. This is typically the name of a valid expression defined within a referenced library, but it may also be a path to a specific data element. The criteria defines the data to be returned for this element.

items.name: text
items.description: Free text dosage can be used for cases where the dosage administered is too complex to code. When coded dosage is present, the free text dosage may still be present for display to humans.The dosage instructions should reflect the dosage of the medication that was administered.

items.name: site
items.description: A coded specification of the anatomic site where the medication first entered the body.  For example, "left arm".

items.name: route
items.description: A code specifying the route or physiological path of administration of a therapeutic agent into or onto the patient.  For example, topical, intravenous, etc.

items.name: method
items.description: A coded value indicating the method by which the medication is intended to be or was introduced into or on the body.  This attribute will most often NOT be populated.  It is most commonly used for injections.  For example, Slow Push, Deep IV.

items.name: dose
items.description: The amount of the medication given at one administration event.   Use this value when the administration is essentially an instantaneous event such as a swallowing a tablet or giving an injection.

items.name: rate[x]
items.description: Identifies the speed with which the medication was or will be introduced into the patient.  Typically, the rate for an infusion e.g. 100 ml per 1 hour or 100 ml/hr.  May also be expressed as a rate per unit of time, e.g. 500 ml per 2 hours.  Other examples:  200 mcg/min or 200 mcg/1 minute; 1 liter/8 hours.

items.name: actor

structure.name: MedicationDispense_Substitution
structure.description: Indicates whether or not substitution was made as part of the dispense.  In some cases, substitution will be expected but does not happen, in other cases substitution is not expected but does happen.  This block explains what substitution did or did not happen and why.  If nothing is specified, substitution was not done.

There may be in some settings (e.g. hospitals) institutional or system support for completing the dispense details in the pharmacy department.

items.name: initialFill
items.description: Indicates the quantity or duration for the first dispense of the medication.

items.name: dispenseInterval
items.description: The minimum period of time that must occur between dispenses of the medication.

items.name: validityPeriod
items.description: This indicates the validity period of a prescription (stale dating the Prescription).

items.name: numberOfRepeatsAllowed
items.description: An integer indicating the number of times, in addition to the original dispense, (aka refills or repeats) that the patient can receive the prescribed medication. Usage Notes: This integer does not include the original order dispense. This means that if an order indicates dispense 30 tablets plus "3 repeats", then the order can be dispensed a total of 4 times and the patient can receive a total of 120 tablets.  A prescriber may explicitly say that zero refills are permitted after the initial dispense.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The amount that is to be dispensed for one fill.

items.name: expectedSupplyDuration
items.description: Identifies the period time over which the supplied product is expected to be used, or the length of time the dispense is expected to last.

items.name: performer
items.description: Indicates the intended dispensing Organization specified by the prescriber.

structure.name: MedicationRequest_Substitution
structure.description: Indicates whether or not substitution can or should be part of the dispense. In some cases, substitution must happen, in other cases substitution must not happen. This block explains the prescriber's intent. If nothing is specified substitution may be done.

items.name: chromosome
items.description: Structural unit composed of a nucleic acid molecule which controls its own replication through the interaction of specific proteins at one or more origins of replication ([SO:0000340](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0000340)).

items.name: genomeBuild
items.description: The Genome Build used for reference, following GRCh build versions e.g. 'GRCh 37'.  Version number must be included if a versioned release of a primary build was used.

items.name: orientation
items.description: A relative reference to a DNA strand based on gene orientation. The strand that contains the open reading frame of the gene is the "sense" strand, and the opposite complementary strand is the "antisense" strand.

items.name: referenceSeqId
items.description: Reference identifier of reference sequence submitted to NCBI. It must match the type in the MolecularSequence.type field. For example, the prefix, “NG_” identifies reference sequence for genes, “NM_” for messenger RNA transcripts, and “NP_” for amino acid sequences.

items.name: referenceSeqPointer
items.description: A pointer to another MolecularSequence entity as reference sequence.

items.name: referenceSeqString
items.description: A string like "ACGT".

items.name: strand
items.description: An absolute reference to a strand. The Watson strand is the strand whose 5'-end is on the short arm of the chromosome, and the Crick strand as the one whose 5'-end is on the long arm.

items.name: windowStart
items.description: Start position of the window on the reference sequence. If the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.

items.name: windowEnd
items.description: End position of the window on the reference sequence. If the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. If the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.

structure.name: MolecularSequence_Variant
structure.description: The definition of variant here originates from Sequence ontology ([variant_of](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/variant_of)). This element can represent amino acid or nucleic sequence change(including insertion,deletion,SNP,etc.)  It can represent some complex mutation or segment variation with the assist of CIGAR string.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: start
items.description: Start position of the variant on the  reference sequence. If the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.

items.name: end
items.description: End position of the variant on the reference sequence. If the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. If the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.

items.name: observedAllele
items.description: An allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene ([SO:0001023](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0001023)).  Nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the observed  sequence. When the sequence  type is DNA, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. This will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end.

items.name: referenceAllele
items.description: An allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene ([SO:0001023](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0001023)). Nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the reference sequence. When the sequence  type is DNA, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. This will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end.

items.name: cigar
items.description: Extended CIGAR string for aligning the sequence with reference bases. See detailed documentation [here](http://support.illumina.com/help/SequencingAnalysisWorkflow/Content/Vault/Informatics/Sequencing_Analysis/CASAVA/swSEQ_mCA_ExtendedCIGARFormat.htm).

items.name: variantPointer
items.description: A pointer to an Observation containing variant information.

structure.name: MolecularSequence_Quality
structure.description: An experimental feature attribute that defines the quality of the feature in a quantitative way, such as a phred quality score ([SO:0001686](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0001686)).

items.name: id

items.name: type
items.description: INDEL / SNP / Undefined variant.

items.name: standardSequence
items.description: Gold standard sequence used for comparing against.

items.name: start
items.description: Start position of the sequence. If the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.

items.name: end
items.description: End position of the sequence. If the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. If the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.

items.name: score
items.description: The score of an experimentally derived feature such as a p-value ([SO:0001685](http://www.sequenceontology.org/browser/current_svn/term/SO:0001685)).

items.name: method
items.description: Which method is used to get sequence quality.

items.name: truthTP
items.description: True positives, from the perspective of the truth data, i.e. the number of sites in the Truth Call Set for which there are paths through the Query Call Set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event.

items.name: queryTP
items.description: True positives, from the perspective of the query data, i.e. the number of sites in the Query Call Set for which there are paths through the Truth Call Set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event.

items.name: truthFN
items.description: False negatives, i.e. the number of sites in the Truth Call Set for which there is no path through the Query Call Set that is consistent with all of the alleles at this site, or sites for which there is an inaccurate genotype call for the event. Sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here.

items.name: queryFP
items.description: False positives, i.e. the number of sites in the Query Call Set for which there is no path through the Truth Call Set that is consistent with this site. Sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here.

items.name: gtFP
items.description: The number of false positives where the non-REF alleles in the Truth and Query Call Sets match (i.e. cases where the truth is 1/1 and the query is 0/1 or similar).

items.name: precision
items.description: QUERY.TP / (QUERY.TP + QUERY.FP).

items.name: recall
items.description: TRUTH.TP / (TRUTH.TP + TRUTH.FN).

items.name: fScore
items.description: Harmonic mean of Recall and Precision, computed as: 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall).

items.name: roc
items.description: Receiver Operator Characteristic (ROC) Curve  to give sensitivity/specificity tradeoff.

items.name: type
