items.name: schedule
items.description: The time period and frequency at which the diet should be given.  The diet should be given for the combination of all schedules if more than one schedule is present.

items.name: nutrient
items.description: Class that defines the quantity and type of nutrient modifications (for example carbohydrate, fiber or sodium) required for the oral diet.

items.name: texture
items.description: Class that describes any texture modifications required for the patient to safely consume various types of solid foods.

items.name: fluidConsistencyType
items.description: The required consistency (e.g. honey-thick, nectar-thick, thin, thickened.) of liquids or fluids served to the patient.

items.name: instruction

items.name: baseFormulaType
items.description: The type of enteral or infant formula such as an adult standard formula with fiber or a soy-based infant formula.

items.name: baseFormulaProductName
items.description: The product or brand name of the enteral or infant formula product such as "ACME Adult Standard Formula".

items.name: additiveType
items.description: Indicates the type of modular component such as protein, carbohydrate, fat or fiber to be provided in addition to or mixed with the base formula.

items.name: additiveProductName
items.description: The product or brand name of the type of modular component to be added to the formula.

items.name: caloricDensity
items.description: The amount of energy (calories) that the formula should provide per specified volume, typically per mL or fluid oz.  For example, an infant may require a formula that provides 24 calories per fluid ounce or an adult may require an enteral formula that provides 1.5 calorie/mL.

items.name: routeofAdministration
items.description: The route or physiological path of administration into the patient's gastrointestinal  tract for purposes of providing the formula feeding, e.g. nasogastric tube.

items.name: administration
items.description: Formula administration instructions as structured data.  This repeating structure allows for changing the administration rate or volume over time for both bolus and continuous feeding.  An example of this would be an instruction to increase the rate of continuous feeding every 2 hours.

items.name: maxVolumeToDeliver
items.description: The maximum total quantity of formula that may be administered to a subject over the period of time, e.g. 1440 mL over 24 hours.

items.name: administrationInstruction
items.description: Free text formula administration, feeding instructions or additional instructions or information.

structure.name: Observation_ReferenceRange
structure.description: Guidance on how to interpret the value by comparison to a normal or recommended range.  Multiple reference ranges are interpreted as an "OR".   In other words, to represent two distinct target populations, two `referenceRange` elements would be used.

items.name: low
items.description: The value of the low bound of the reference range.  The low bound of the reference range endpoint is inclusive of the value (e.g.  reference range is >=5 - <=9). If the low bound is omitted,  it is assumed to be meaningless (e.g. reference range is <=2.3).

items.name: high
items.description: The value of the high bound of the reference range.  The high bound of the reference range endpoint is inclusive of the value (e.g.  reference range is >=5 - <=9). If the high bound is omitted,  it is assumed to be meaningless (e.g. reference range is >= 2.3).

items.name: type
items.description: Codes to indicate the what part of the targeted reference population it applies to. For example, the normal or therapeutic range.

items.name: appliesTo
items.description: Codes to indicate the target population this reference range applies to.  For example, a reference range may be based on the normal population or a particular sex or race.  Multiple `appliesTo`  are interpreted as an "AND" of the target populations.  For example, to represent a target population of African American females, both a code of female and a code for African American would be used.

items.name: age
items.description: The age at which this reference range is applicable. This is a neonatal age (e.g. number of weeks at term) if the meaning says so.

items.name: text
items.description: Text based reference range in an observation which may be used when a quantitative range is not appropriate for an observation.  An example would be a reference value of "Negative" or a list or table of "normals".

structure.name: Observation_Component
structure.description: Some observations have multiple component observations.  These component observations are expressed as separate code value pairs that share the same attributes.  Examples include systolic and diastolic component observations for blood pressure measurement and multiple component observations for genetics observations.

items.name: definition
items.description:
This element is a URI that refers to an [ElementDefinition](elementdefinition.html) that provides information about this item, including information that might otherwise be included in the instance of the Questionnaire resource. A detailed description of the construction of the URI is shown in Comments, below. If this element is present then the following element values MAY be derived from the Element Definition if the corresponding elements of this Questionnaire resource instance have no value:

* code (ElementDefinition.code) 
* type (ElementDefinition.type) 
* required (ElementDefinition.min) 
* repeats (ElementDefinition.max) 
* maxLength (ElementDefinition.maxLength) 
* answerValueSet (ElementDefinition.binding)
* options (ElementDefinition.binding).

items.name: code
items.description: A terminology code that corresponds to this group or question (e.g. a code from LOINC, which defines many questions and answers).

items.name: prefix
items.description: A short label for a particular group, question or set of display text within the questionnaire used for reference by the individual completing the questionnaire.

items.name: text
items.description: The name of a section, the text of a question or text content for a display item.

items.name: type
items.description: The type of questionnaire item this is - whether text for display, a grouping of other items or a particular type of data to be captured (string, integer, coded choice, etc.).

items.name: enableWhen
items.description: A constraint indicating that this item should only be enabled (displayed/allow answers to be captured) when the specified condition is true.

items.name: enableBehavior
items.description: Controls how multiple enableWhen values are interpreted -  whether all or any must be true.

items.name: required
items.description: An indication, if true, that the item must be present in a "completed" QuestionnaireResponse.  If false, the item may be skipped when answering the questionnaire.

items.name: repeats
items.description: An indication, if true, that the item may occur multiple times in the response, collecting multiple answers for questions or multiple sets of answers for groups.

items.name: readOnly
items.description: An indication, when true, that the value cannot be changed by a human respondent to the Questionnaire.

items.name: maxLength
items.description: The maximum number of characters that are permitted in the answer to be considered a "valid" QuestionnaireResponse.

items.name: answerValueSet

items.name: answerOption
items.description: One of the permitted answers for a "choice" or "open-choice" question.

items.name: initial
items.description: One or more values that should be pre-populated in the answer when initially rendering the questionnaire for user input.

items.name: item
items.description: Text, questions and other groups to be nested beneath a question or group.

items.name: outcome
items.description: One of the potential outcomes for the patient (e.g. remission, death,  a particular condition).

items.name: probability[x]
items.description: Indicates how likely the outcome is (in the specified timeframe).

items.name: qualitativeRisk
items.description: Indicates how likely the outcome is (in the specified timeframe), expressed as a qualitative value (e.g. low, medium, or high).

items.name: relativeRisk
items.description: Indicates the risk for this particular subject (with their specific characteristics) divided by the risk of the population in general.  (Numbers greater than 1 = higher risk than the population, numbers less than 1 = lower risk.).

items.name: when[x]
items.description: Indicates the period of time or age range of the subject to which the specified probability applies.

items.name: rationale
items.description: Additional information explaining the basis for the prediction.

items.name: subunit
items.description: Index of linear sequences of nucleic acids in order of decreasing length. Sequences of the same length will be ordered by molecular weight. Subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated and have sequential subscripts.

items.name: sequence
items.description: Actual nucleotide sequence notation from 5' to 3' end using standard single letter codes. In addition to the base sequence, sugar and type of phosphate or non-phosphate linkage should also be captured.

items.name: length
items.description: The length of the sequence shall be captured.

items.name: sequenceAttachment
items.description: (TBC).

items.name: fivePrime
items.description: The nucleotide present at the 5’ terminal shall be specified based on a controlled vocabulary. Since the sequence is represented from the 5' to the 3' end, the 5’ prime nucleotide is the letter at the first position in the sequence. A separate representation would be redundant.

items.name: threePrime
items.description: The nucleotide present at the 3’ terminal shall be specified based on a controlled vocabulary. Since the sequence is represented from the 5' to the 3' end, the 5’ prime nucleotide is the letter at the last position in the sequence. A separate representation would be redundant.

items.name: linkage
items.description: The linkages between sugar residues will also be captured.

items.name: sugar
items.description: 5.3.6.8.1 Sugar ID (Mandatory).

structure.name: SubstanceProtein_Subunit
structure.description: This subclause refers to the description of each subunit constituting the SubstanceProtein. A subunit is a linear sequence of amino acids linked through peptide bonds. The Subunit information shall be provided when the finished SubstanceProtein is a complex of multiple sequences; subunits are not used to delineate domains within a single sequence. Subunits are listed in order of decreasing length; sequences of the same length will be ordered by decreasing molecular weight; subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated multiple times.

items.name: subunit
items.description: Index of primary sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds in order of decreasing length. Sequences of the same length will be ordered by molecular weight. Subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated and have sequential subscripts.

items.name: sequence
items.description: The sequence information shall be provided enumerating the amino acids from N- to C-terminal end using standard single-letter amino acid codes. Uppercase shall be used for L-amino acids and lowercase for D-amino acids. Transcribed SubstanceProteins will always be described using the translated sequence; for synthetic peptide containing amino acids that are not represented with a single letter code an X should be used within the sequence. The modified amino acids will be distinguished by their position in the sequence.

items.name: length
items.description: Length of linear sequences of amino acids contained in the subunit.

items.name: sequenceAttachment
items.description: The sequence information shall be provided enumerating the amino acids from N- to C-terminal end using standard single-letter amino acid codes. Uppercase shall be used for L-amino acids and lowercase for D-amino acids. Transcribed SubstanceProteins will always be described using the translated sequence; for synthetic peptide containing amino acids that are not represented with a single letter code an X should be used within the sequence. The modified amino acids will be distinguished by their position in the sequence.

items.name: cTerminalModificationId
items.description: Unique identifier for molecular fragment modification based on the ISO 11238 Substance ID.

items.name: cTerminalModification
items.description: The modification at the C-terminal shall be specified.

structure.name: SubstanceSourceMaterial_FractionDescription
structure.description: Many complex materials are fractions of parts of plants, animals, or minerals. Fraction elements are often necessary to define both Substances and Specified Group 1 Substances. For substances derived from Plants, fraction information will be captured at the Substance information level ( . Oils, Juices and Exudates). Additional information for Extracts, such as extraction solvent composition, will be captured at the Specified Substance Group 1 information level. For plasma-derived products fraction information will be captured at the Substance and the Specified Substance Group 1 levels.

items.name: family
items.description: The family of an organism shall be specified.

items.name: genus
items.description: The genus of an organism shall be specified; refers to the Latin epithet of the genus element of the plant/animal scientific name; it is present in names for genera, species and infraspecies.

items.name: species
items.description: The species of an organism shall be specified; refers to the Latin epithet of the species of the plant/animal; it is present in names for species and infraspecies.

items.name: intraspecificType
items.description: The Intraspecific type of an organism shall be specified.

items.name: intraspecificDescription
items.description: The intraspecific description of an organism shall be specified based on a controlled vocabulary. For Influenza Vaccine, the intraspecific description shall contain the syntax of the antigen in line with the WHO convention.

items.name: hybrid

items.name: kind
items.description: A description of the kind of resource the in-line definition of a care plan activity is representing.  The CarePlan.activity.detail is an in-line definition when a resource is not referenced using CarePlan.activity.reference.  For example, a MedicationRequest, a ServiceRequest, or a CommunicationRequest.

items.name: instantiatesCanonical
items.description: The URL pointing to a FHIR-defined protocol, guideline, questionnaire or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this CarePlan activity.

items.name: instantiatesUri
items.description: The URL pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, questionnaire or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this CarePlan activity.

items.name: code
items.description: Detailed description of the type of planned activity; e.g. what lab test, what procedure, what kind of encounter.

items.name: reasonCode
items.description: Provides the rationale that drove the inclusion of this particular activity as part of the plan or the reason why the activity was prohibited.

items.name: reasonReference
items.description: Indicates another resource, such as the health condition(s), whose existence justifies this request and drove the inclusion of this particular activity as part of the plan.

items.name: goal
items.description: Internal reference that identifies the goals that this activity is intended to contribute towards meeting.

items.name: status
items.description: Identifies what progress is being made for the specific activity.

items.name: statusReason
items.description: Provides reason why the activity isn't yet started, is on hold, was cancelled, etc.

items.name: doNotPerform
items.description: If true, indicates that the described activity is one that must NOT be engaged in when following the plan.  If false, or missing, indicates that the described activity is one that should be engaged in when following the plan.

items.name: scheduled[x]
items.description: The period, timing or frequency upon which the described activity is to occur.

items.name: location
items.description: Identifies the facility where the activity will occur; e.g. home, hospital, specific clinic, etc.

items.name: performer
items.description: Identifies who's expected to be involved in the activity.

items.name: product[x]
items.description: Identifies the food, drug or other product to be consumed or supplied in the activity.

items.name: dailyAmount
items.description: Identifies the quantity expected to be consumed in a given day.

items.name: quantity
items.description: Identifies the quantity expected to be supplied, administered or consumed by the subject.

items.name: description
items.description: This provides a textual description of constraints on the intended activity occurrence, including relation to other activities.  It may also include objectives, pre-conditions and end-conditions.  Finally, it may convey specifics about the activity such as body site, method, route, etc.

structure.name: ChargeItemDefinition_PropertyGroup_PriceComponent
structure.description: The price for a ChargeItem may be calculated as a base price with surcharges/deductions that apply in certain conditions. A ChargeItemDefinition resource that defines the prices, factors and conditions that apply to a billing code is currently under development. The priceComponent element can be used to offer transparency to the recipient of the Invoice of how the prices have been calculated.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify item entries.

items.name: revenue
items.description: The type of revenue or cost center providing the product and/or service.

items.name: category
items.description: Code to identify the general type of benefits under which products and services are provided.

items.name: productOrService
items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.name: modifier
items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: programCode
items.description: Identifies the program under which this may be recovered.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: udi
items.description: Unique Device Identifiers associated with this line item.

items.name: subDetail
items.description: A claim detail line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of sub-details which are simple items.

items.name: category
items.description: A code to indicate the information type of this adjudication record. Information types may include the value submitted, maximum values or percentages allowed or payable under the plan, amounts that: the patient is responsible for in aggregate or pertaining to this item; amounts paid by other coverages; and, the benefit payable for this item.

items.name: amount
items.description: Monetary amount associated with the category.

items.name: value

structure.name: Invoice_LineItem_PriceComponent

The priceComponent element can be used to offer transparency to the recipient of the Invoice as to how the prices have been calculated.

items.name: connectivity
items.description: The entity that links the sugar residues together should also be captured for nearly all naturally occurring nucleic acid the linkage is a phosphate group. For many synthetic oligonucleotides phosphorothioate linkages are often seen. Linkage connectivity is assumed to be 3’-5’. If the linkage is either 3’-3’ or 5’-5’ this should be specified.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Each linkage will be registered as a fragment and have an ID.

items.name: name
items.description: Each linkage will be registered as a fragment and have at least one name. A single name shall be assigned to each linkage.

items.name: residueSite
items.description: Residues shall be captured as described in 5.3.6.8.3.

items.name: authorType
items.description: The type of author of an organism species shall be specified. The parenthetical author of an organism species refers to the first author who published the plant/animal name (of any rank). The primary author of an organism species refers to the first author(s), who validly published the plant/animal name.

items.name: authorDescription
items.description: The author of an organism species shall be specified. The author year of an organism shall also be specified when applicable; refers to the year in which the first author(s) published the infraspecific plant/animal name (of any rank).

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify item entries.

items.description: The type of revenue or cost center providing the product and/or service.

items.description: Code to identify the general type of benefits under which products and services are provided.

items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: programCode
items.description: Identifies the program under which this may be recovered.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: udi
items.description: Unique Device Identifiers associated with this line item.

items.name: entity[x]
items.description: Specific type of Contract Valued Item that may be priced.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Identifies a Contract Valued Item instance.

items.name: effectiveTime
items.description: Indicates the time during which this Contract ValuedItem information is effective.

items.name: quantity
items.description: Specifies the units by which the Contract Valued Item is measured or counted, and quantifies the countable or measurable Contract Valued Item instances.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: A Contract Valued Item unit valuation measure.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of the Contract Valued Item delivered. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: points
items.description: An amount that expresses the weighting (based on difficulty, cost and/or resource intensiveness) associated with the Contract Valued Item delivered. The concept of Points allows for assignment of point values for a Contract Valued Item, such that a monetary amount can be assigned to each point.

items.name: net
items.description: Expresses the product of the Contract Valued Item unitQuantity and the unitPriceAmt. For example, the formula: unit Quantity * unit Price (Cost per Point) * factor Number  * points = net Amount. Quantity, factor and points are assumed to be 1 if not supplied.

items.name: payment
items.description: Terms of valuation.

items.name: paymentDate
items.description: When payment is due.

items.name: responsible
items.description: Who will make payment.

items.name: recipient

items.name: method
items.description: The HTTP method the test engine MUST use for this operation regardless of any other operation details.

items.name: origin

items.description: Header elements would be used to set HTTP headers.

items.description: The id of the fixture used as the body of a PUT or POST request.

items.name: targetId
items.description: Id of fixture used for extracting the [id],  [type], and [vid] for GET requests.

items.name: url
items.description: Complete request URL.

items.description: Id of the source fixture used as the contents to be evaluated by either the "source/expression" or "sourceId/path" definition.

items.description: The FHIRPath expression to evaluate against the source fixture. When compareToSourceId is defined, either compareToSourceExpression or compareToSourcePath must be defined, but not both.

items.description: The FHIRPath expression to be evaluated against the request or response message contents - HTTP headers and payload.

items.name: headerField
items.description: The HTTP header field name e.g. 'Location'.

items.description: The ID of a fixture.  Asserts that the response contains at a minimum the fixture specified by minimumId.

items.name: navigationLinks
items.description: Whether or not the test execution performs validation on the bundle navigation links.

items.name: operator
items.description: The operator type defines the conditional behavior of the assert. If not defined, the default is equals.

items.name: path

items.name: requestMethod
items.description: The request method or HTTP operation code to compare against that used by the client system under test.

items.name: requestURL
items.description: The value to use in a comparison against the request URL path string.

items.name: resource
items.description: The type of the resource.  See http://build.fhir.org/resourcelist.html.

items.name: response
items.description: okay | created | noContent | notModified | bad | forbidden | notFound | methodNotAllowed | conflict | gone | preconditionFailed | unprocessable.

items.name: responseCode
items.description: The value of the HTTP response code to be tested.

items.name: sourceId
items.description: Fixture to evaluate the XPath/JSONPath expression or the headerField  against.

items.name: validateProfileId
items.description: The ID of the Profile to validate against.

items.name: value
items.description: The value to compare to.

items.name: warningOnly

structure.name: Address
structure.description: Base StructureDefinition for Address Type: An address expressed using postal conventions (as opposed to GPS or other location definition formats).  This data type may be used to convey addresses for use in delivering mail as well as for visiting locations which might not be valid for mail delivery.  There are a variety of postal address formats defined around the world.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: use
items.description: The purpose of this address.

items.name: type
items.description: Distinguishes between physical addresses (those you can visit) and mailing addresses (e.g. PO Boxes and care-of addresses). Most addresses are both.

items.name: text
items.description: Specifies the entire address as it should be displayed e.g. on a postal label. This may be provided instead of or as well as the specific parts.

items.name: line
items.description: This component contains the house number, apartment number, street name, street direction,  P.O. Box number, delivery hints, and similar address information.

items.name: city
items.description: The name of the city, town, suburb, village or other community or delivery center.

items.name: district
items.description: The name of the administrative area (county).

items.name: state
items.description: Sub-unit of a country with limited sovereignty in a federally organized country. A code may be used if codes are in common use (e.g. US 2 letter state codes).

items.name: postalCode
items.description: A postal code designating a region defined by the postal service.

items.name: country
items.description: Country - a nation as commonly understood or generally accepted.

items.name: period
items.description: Time period when address was/is in use.

items.name: mustSupport
items.description:
Indicates that specific elements of the type are referenced by the knowledge module and must be supported by the consumer in order to obtain an effective evaluation. This does not mean that a value is required for this element, only that the consuming system must understand the element and be able to provide values for it if they are available. 

The value of mustSupport SHALL be a FHIRPath resolveable on the type of the DataRequirement. The path SHALL consist only of identifiers, constant indexers, and .resolve() (see the [Simple FHIRPath Profile](fhirpath.html#simple) for full details).

items.name: codeFilter
items.description: Code filters specify additional constraints on the data, specifying the value set of interest for a particular element of the data. Each code filter defines an additional constraint on the data, i.e. code filters are AND'ed, not OR'ed.

items.name: dateFilter
items.description: Date filters specify additional constraints on the data in terms of the applicable date range for specific elements. Each date filter specifies an additional constraint on the data, i.e. date filters are AND'ed, not OR'ed.

items.name: limit
items.description: Specifies a maximum number of results that are required (uses the _count search parameter).

items.name: sort
items.description: Specifies the order of the results to be returned.

Slicing can be used in any resource that has cardinality ..* on the base resource, or any resource with a choice of types. The set of slices is any elements that come after this in the element sequence that have the same path, until a shorter path occurs (the shorter path terminates the set).

items.description: The maximum number of times this element is permitted to appear in the instance.

items.description: Information about the base definition of the element, provided to make it unnecessary for tools to trace the deviation of the element through the derived and related profiles. When the element definition is not the original definition of an element - i.g. either in a constraint on another type, or for elements from a super type in a snap shot - then the information in provided in the element definition may be different to the base definition. On the original definition of the element, it will be same.

items.description: The Implicit meaning that is to be understood when this element is missing (e.g. 'when this element is missing, the period is ongoing').

items.name: orderMeaning
items.description: If present, indicates that the order of the repeating element has meaning and describes what that meaning is.  If absent, it means that the order of the element has no meaning.

items.name: fixed[x]
items.description: Specifies a value that SHALL be exactly the value  for this element in the instance. For purposes of comparison, non-significant whitespace is ignored, and all values must be an exact match (case and accent sensitive). Missing elements/attributes must also be missing.

items.name: pattern[x]
items.description:
Specifies a value that the value in the instance SHALL follow - that is, any value in the pattern must be found in the instance. Other additional values may be found too. This is effectively constraint by example.  

When pattern[x] is used to constrain a primitive, it means that the value provided in the pattern[x] must match the instance value exactly.

When pattern[x] is used to constrain an array, it means that each element provided in the pattern[x] array must (recursively) match at least one element from the instance array.

When pattern[x] is used to constrain a complex object, it means that each property in the pattern must be present in the complex object, and its value must recursively match -- i.e.,

1. If primitive: it must match exactly the pattern value
2. If a complex object: it must match (recursively) the pattern value
3. If an array: it must match (recursively) the pattern value.

items.name: maxValue[x]
items.description: The maximum allowed value for the element. The value is inclusive. This is allowed for the types date, dateTime, instant, time, decimal, integer, and Quantity.

items.description: A correction factor that is applied to the sampled data points before they are added to the origin.

items.description: The lower limit of detection of the measured points. This is needed if any of the data points have the value "L" (lower than detection limit).

items.name: upperLimit

structure.description: Code filters specify additional constraints on the data, specifying the value set of interest for a particular element of the data. Each code filter defines an additional constraint on the data, i.e. code filters are AND'ed, not OR'ed.

Note that the index must be an integer constant. The path must resolve to an element of type code, Coding, or CodeableConcept.

If one or more discriminators are provided, the value of the child elements in the instance data SHALL completely distinguish which slice the element in the resource matches based on the allowed values for those elements in each of the slices.

items.description: A human-readable text description of how the slicing works. If there is no discriminator, this is required to be present to provide whatever information is possible about how the slices can be differentiated.

items.name: ordered
items.description: If the matching elements have to occur in the same order as defined in the profile.

items.name: rules
items.description: Whether additional slices are allowed or not. When the slices are ordered, profile authors can also say that additional slices are only allowed at the end.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Base
structure.description: Information about the base definition of the element, provided to make it unnecessary for tools to trace the deviation of the element through the derived and related profiles. When the element definition is not the original definition of an element - i.g. either in a constraint on another type, or for elements from a super type in a snap shot - then the information in provided in the element definition may be different to the base definition. On the original definition of the element, it will be same.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: path
items.description: The Path that identifies the base element - this matches the ElementDefinition.path for that element. Across FHIR, there is only one base definition of any element - that is, an element definition on a [StructureDefinition](structuredefinition.html#) without a StructureDefinition.base.

items.name: min
items.description: Minimum cardinality of the base element identified by the path.

items.name: max
items.description: Maximum cardinality of the base element identified by the path.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Type
structure.description: The data type or resource that the value of this element is permitted to be.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: code
items.description: URL of Data type or Resource that is a(or the) type used for this element. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition e.g. "string" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/string. Absolute URLs are only allowed in logical models.

items.name: profile
items.description: Identifies a profile structure or implementation Guide that applies to the datatype this element refers to. If any profiles are specified, then the content must conform to at least one of them. The URL can be a local reference - to a contained StructureDefinition, or a reference to another StructureDefinition or Implementation Guide by a canonical URL. When an implementation guide is specified, the type SHALL conform to at least one profile defined in the implementation guide.

items.name: targetProfile
items.description: Used when the type is "Reference" or "canonical", and identifies a profile structure or implementation Guide that applies to the target of the reference this element refers to. If any profiles are specified, then the content must conform to at least one of them. The URL can be a local reference - to a contained StructureDefinition, or a reference to another StructureDefinition or Implementation Guide by a canonical URL. When an implementation guide is specified, the target resource SHALL conform to at least one profile defined in the implementation guide.

items.name: aggregation
items.description: If the type is a reference to another resource, how the resource is or can be aggregated - is it a contained resource, or a reference, and if the context is a bundle, is it included in the bundle.

items.name: versioning
items.description: Whether this reference needs to be version specific or version independent, or whether either can be used.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Example
structure.description: A sample value for this element demonstrating the type of information that would typically be found in the element.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: label
items.description: Describes the purpose of this example amoung the set of examples.

items.name: value[x]
items.description: The actual value for the element, which must be one of the types allowed for this element.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Constraint
structure.description: Formal constraints such as co-occurrence and other constraints that can be computationally evaluated within the context of the instance.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: key
items.description: Allows identification of which elements have their cardinalities impacted by the constraint.  Will not be referenced for constraints that do not affect cardinality.

items.name: requirements
items.description: Description of why this constraint is necessary or appropriate.

items.name: severity
items.description: Identifies the impact constraint violation has on the conformance of the instance.

items.name: human
items.description: Text that can be used to describe the constraint in messages identifying that the constraint has been violated.

items.name: expression
items.description: A [FHIRPath](fhirpath.html) expression of constraint that can be executed to see if this constraint is met.

items.name: xpath
items.description: An XPath expression of constraint that can be executed to see if this constraint is met.

items.name: source
items.description: A reference to the original source of the constraint, for traceability purposes.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Binding
structure.description: Binds to a value set if this element is coded (code, Coding, CodeableConcept, Quantity), or the data types (string, uri).

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: strength
items.description: Indicates the degree of conformance expectations associated with this binding - that is, the degree to which the provided value set must be adhered to in the instances.

items.name: description
items.description: Describes the intended use of this particular set of codes.

items.name: valueSet
items.description: Refers to the value set that identifies the set of codes the binding refers to.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Mapping
structure.description: Identifies a concept from an external specification that roughly corresponds to this element.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: identity
items.description: An internal reference to the definition of a mapping.

items.name: language
items.description: Identifies the computable language in which mapping.map is expressed.

items.name: map
items.description: Expresses what part of the target specification corresponds to this element.

items.name: comment
items.description: Comments that provide information about the mapping or its use.

structure.name: SubstanceAmount_ReferenceRange
structure.description: Reference range of possible or expected values.

items.name: id

To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. items.name: lowLimit
items.description: Lower limit possible or expected.

items.name: highLimit
items.description: Upper limit possible or expected.

structure.name: Timing_Repeat
structure.description: A set of rules that describe when the event is scheduled.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: bounds[x]
items.description: Either a duration for the length of the timing schedule, a range of possible length, or outer bounds for start and/or end limits of the timing schedule.

items.name: count
items.description: A total count of the desired number of repetitions across the duration of the entire timing specification. If countMax is present, this element indicates the lower bound of the allowed range of count values.

items.name: countMax
items.description: If present, indicates that the count is a range - so to perform the action between [count] and [countMax] times.

items.name: duration
items.description: How long this thing happens for when it happens. If durationMax is present, this element indicates the lower bound of the allowed range of the duration.

items.name: durationMax
items.description: If present, indicates that the duration is a range - so to perform the action between [duration] and [durationMax] time length.

items.name: durationUnit
items.description: The units of time for the duration, in UCUM units.

items.name: frequency
items.description: The number of times to repeat the action within the specified period. If frequencyMax is present, this element indicates the lower bound of the allowed range of the frequency.

items.name: frequencyMax
items.description: If present, indicates that the frequency is a range - so to repeat between [frequency] and [frequencyMax] times within the period or period range.

items.name: period
items.description: Indicates the duration of time over which repetitions are to occur; e.g. to express "3 times per day", 3 would be the frequency and "1 day" would be the period. If periodMax is present, this element indicates the lower bound of the allowed range of the period length.

items.name: periodMax
items.description: If present, indicates that the period is a range from [period] to [periodMax], allowing expressing concepts such as "do this once every 3-5 days.

items.name: periodUnit
items.description: The units of time for the period in UCUM units.

items.name: dayOfWeek
items.description: If one or more days of week is provided, then the action happens only on the specified day(s).

items.name: timeOfDay
items.description: Specified time of day for action to take place.

items.name: when
items.description: An approximate time period during the day, potentially linked to an event of daily living that indicates when the action should occur.

items.name: offset
items.description: The number of minutes from the event. If the event code does not indicate whether the minutes is before or after the event, then the offset is assumed to be after the event.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Slicing_Discriminator
structure.description: Designates which child elements are used to discriminate between the slices when processing an instance. If one or more discriminators are provided, the value of the child elements in the instance data SHALL completely distinguish which slice the element in the resource matches based on the allowed values for those elements in each of the slices.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: type
items.description: How the element value is interpreted when discrimination is evaluated.

items.name: path
items.description: A FHIRPath expression, using [the simple subset of FHIRPath](fhirpath.html#simple), that is used to identify the element on which discrimination is based.
